Creatures.00dpnnui.Name: Zombie
Languages: understands the languages they knew in life but can’t speak

Traits.Name: Negative Nerves
Traits.Content: Whenever a creature deals 5 damage or less to the zombie, the zombie takes no damage instead.

Actions.Name: Slam
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) bludgeoning damage.

Reactions.Name: Delayed Reflex
Reactions.Content: When the zombie’s Negative Nerves trait allows them to ignore damage dealt by a creature the zombie can see within 5 feet of them, the zombie makes a Slam attack with disadvantage against the creature.

Traits.Name: Necrotic Absorption
Traits.Content: Whenever Mycete is subjected to necrotic damage, she takes no damage and instead regains a number of hit points equal to the necrotic damage dealt.

Traits.Name: Utility Spells
Traits.Content:
In addition to any other spells in this stat block, Mycete can cast the following spells, using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13):
- **At will:** druidcraft
- **3/day:** locate animals or plants
- **1/day each:** fog cloud, pass without trace

Actions.Name: Parasitic Spores
Actions.Content: Melee or Ranged Spell Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (2d6) necrotic damage, and if the target is a creature, Mycete regains 3 (1d6) hit points.

Actions.Name: Fungal Growth
Actions.Content: Mycete causes fungi to proliferate from a point she can see within 60 feet of her. Solid surfaces in a 10-foot square centered on that point become difficult terrain, and a creature who enters that area for the first time on a turn or starts their turn there must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn).

Actions.Name: Animate Husks (1/Day)
Actions.Content: Mycete animates three rotting zombies, who appear in unoccupied spaces Mycete can see within 30 feet of her. The zombies act immediately after Mycete on the same initiative count and follow her verbal orders. If Mycete dies, the zombies are destroyed.

BonusActions.Name: Spore Eruption
BonusActions.Content: Mycete destroys a rotting zombie under her control, and the zombie violently erupts with toxic spores. Each enemy within 5 feet of the zombie must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw, taking 9 (2d8) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Creatures.066canm7.Name: Shardbound Acolyte

Traits.Name: Shardbound Madness
Traits.Content: When the Shardbound Acolyte is reduced to 0 hit points, roll a d6. On a 1–3, it dies. On a 4–5, the shard erupts in psychic energy; each creature within 10 feet must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or take 11 (3d6) psychic damage. On a 6, the shard desperately clings to life and the acolyte instead drops to 1 hit point (once per combat).

Traits.Name: Dream-Warped Casting
Traits.Content: The acolyte does not cast spells in a traditional way; reality ripples and bends around it. Its spellcasting cannot be countered by counterspell or similar effects, and it ignores magical effects that specifically prevent spells from being cast, though effects that silence, restrain, or otherwise limit actions still affect it normally.

Traits.Name: Innate Nightmare Magic
Traits.Content:
The acolyte's spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 14, +6 to hit with spell attacks). It can innately cast the following spells, requiring no material components:

At will: detect thoughts
3/day each: dissonant whispers (3rd-level), hold person, blindness/deafness

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The Shardbound Acolyte makes two Nightmare Bolt or Nightmare Pulse attacks.

Actions.Name: Nightmare Bolt
Actions.Content: Ranged Spell Attack: +6 to hit, range 120 ft., one creature. Hit: 14 (3d8 + 1) psychic damage. The target must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or has disadvantage on all attack rolls until the end of its next turn.

Actions.Name: Nightmare Pulse
Actions.Content: Ranged Spell Attack: +6 to hit, range 60 ft., one creature. Hit: 10 (2d8 + 1) psychic damage. The target must succeed on a DC 14 Strength saving throw or be pushed 15 feet away from the acolyte.

Actions.Name: Nightmare Screech (Recharge 5–6)
Actions.Content: The acolyte releases a warped, reality-fracturing cry through the shard in its flesh. Each creature of the acolyte's choice within 20 feet must make a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or have its speed halved and be unable to take reactions until the end of its next turn.

BonusActions.Name: Corrupted Shift
BonusActions.Content: The Shardbound Acolyte teleports up to 10 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. This movement represents the acolyte slipping briefly through the dream-layer of Mythranos and does not provoke opportunity attacks.

Creatures.066canm7.Description: A devoted vessel of nightmare, the Shardbound Acolyte stands at the back of the fray, warping minds and space itself as the Pale Night's whispers bleed through the crack in Creation’s dream.

Creatures.08ryrgz3.Name: Zagren the Ruthless
DamageImmunities: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical weapons that aren't silvered
Languages: the languages it knew in life

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The grave knight makes two greatsword attacks. It can use its Life Drain in place of one greatsword attack.

Actions.Name: Life Drain
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 4) necrotic damage. The target must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or its hit point maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the damage taken. This reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest. The target dies if this effect reduces its hit point maximum to 0.

Actions.Name: +2 Greatsword
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 6) slashing damage.

LegendaryActions.Name: Grave Step
LegendaryActions.Content: Zagren teleports up to 15 feet to an unoccupied space he can see. His form briefly becomes a swirl of bones and barnacles.

LegendaryActions.Name: Dreadful Command (Costs 2 Actions)
LegendaryActions.Content: Zagren targets one creature that can see or hear him within 30 feet. It must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or be frightened until the end of its next turn. If already frightened, it instead falls prone.

LegendaryActions.Name: Drag to the Deep
LegendaryActions.Content: Zagren lashes out with ghostly chains formed from seaweed and anchor fragments. One creature within 20 feet must succeed on a DC 13 Strength saving throw or be pulled 20 feet closer and take 5 (1d10) necrotic damage. The target is restrained until the end of Zagren’s next turn.

MythicActions.Name: Tide of the Dead
MythicActions.Content: Barnacle-crusted skeletal arms rise from the sodden ground. Up to three creatures of Zagren’s choice within 30 feet must succeed on a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw or be restrained until the end of their next turn as the arms clutch at them.

MythicActions.Name: Ghostwind Moan
MythicActions.Content: A chorus of drowned whispers sweeps through the tomb. All non-undead creatures must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or suffer disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of their next turn.

MythicActions.Name: Saltwater Surge
MythicActions.Content: A sudden wave of briny water bursts from the walls. Creatures in the room must make a DC 14 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone and pushed 10 feet away. The area becomes difficult terrain until the end of the next round.

Creatures.08ryrgz3.Description: Once a feared pirate captain, Zagren the Ruthless now lingers as an undead Grave Knight. His body is encrusted with barnacles and seaweed, his armor corroded by the sea, and his sword swings with the weight of his cursed oath to rule death as he once ruled the seas.

Creatures.0gppjbmh.Name: Shardbound Nightmare
DamageResistances: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks

Traits.Name: Almost Unmoored
Traits.Content: The Nightmare-Touched can move along walls and ceilings as if they were normal ground, ignoring difficult terrain caused by vertical surfaces.

Traits.Name: Corruption Aura
Traits.Content: A creature that starts its turn within 10 feet of the Nightmare-Touched must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take 5 (1d10) psychic damage.

Traits.Name: Reality Flicker
Traits.Content: When the Nightmare-Touched is targeted by an attack, it can shift 5 feet as a reaction. If this movement takes it out of reach, the triggering attack misses.

Traits.Name: Dream-Shear Critical
Traits.Content: When the Nightmare-Touched scores a critical hit, it deals an additional 10 (3d6) psychic damage and the target must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be dazed on its next turn.

Traits.Name: Shardbound Madness
Traits.Content: When the Nightmare-Touched is reduced to 0 hit points, the embedded shard detonates. Each creature within 15 feet must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw, taking 14 (4d6) psychic damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The Nightmare-Touched makes two Dark-Claw attacks, or one Dark-Claw attack and one Rend Reality attack if available.

Actions.Name: Dark-Claw
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) psychic damage. If the Nightmare-Touched teleported this turn, the attack deals an extra 1d8 psychic damage.

Actions.Name: Rend Reality (Recharge 4–6)
Actions.Content: The Nightmare-Touched tears a jagged seam in space. Each creature in a 15-foot line must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking 22 (4d10) psychic damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. On a failed save, the creature’s next teleport or magical movement ability is suppressed until the end of its next turn as reality resists further distortion.

Actions.Name: Nightmare Screech (Recharge 5–6)
Actions.Content: The Nightmare-Touched releases a chorus of overlapping, impossible sounds. Each creature of its choice within 20 feet must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or have its speed halved and be unable to take reactions until the end of its next turn.

BonusActions.Name: Corrupted Shift
BonusActions.Content: The Nightmare-Touched teleports up to 20 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. After teleporting, it gains +2 AC until the start of its next turn as its form phases between moments.

Reactions.Name: Reality Flicker
Reactions.Content: The Nightmare shifts 5 feet when targeted by an attack as a reaction. If this takes the Nightmare out of range the attack misses, otherwise the attack is made with disadvantage.

Creatures.0gppjbmh.Description: A vessel teetering on the brink of true nightmare form, the Shardbound Nightmare slips through cracks in reality as easily as it moves across walls and ceilings. Its attacks distort space, its presence corrodes the mind, and each movement leaves afterimages that gnaw at mortal perception.

Traits.Name: Ambusher
Traits.Content: In the first round of combat, the scoundrel has advantage on attack rolls against any surprised creature.

Traits.Name: Exploit Opening (3/Day)
Traits.Content: When the scoundrel makes an attack, they have advantage on the attack roll.

Traits.Name: Hit ‘Em Where It Hurts
Traits.Content: When the scoundrel has advantage on a weapon attack roll, the attack deals an extra 7 (2d6) damage on a hit.

Actions.Name: Rapier
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage, and the scoundrel can make a dagger attack against the target with advantage.

Actions.Name: Dagger
Actions.Content: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) piercing damage.

Creatures.0yti34mg.Name: Haunt
DamageResistances: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from mundane attacks

Traits.Name: Incorporeal Cloud
Traits.Content: The haunt can occupy another creature’s space and vice versa. In addition, the haunt can move through creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. The haunt takes 5 (1d10) force damage if they end their turn inside an object.

Traits.Name: Invisibility
Traits.Content: The haunt is invisible.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The haunt makes two Spectral Wrath attacks.

Actions.Name: Spectral Wrath
Actions.Content: Melee Spell Attack: +8 to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the haunt’s space. Hit: 17 (2d12 + 4) force damage.

Actions.Name: Wave of Despair (Recharge 5–6)
Actions.Content: The haunt unleashes a wave of psychic pain. Each enemy within 20 feet of the haunt must make a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw, taking 33 (6d10) psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

BonusActions.Name: Possess Object
BonusActions.Content:
The haunt can magically manipulate a Large or smaller object within 30 feet of them that isn’t being worn or carried by another creature. The haunt can exert fine control on objects under their control, such as playing piano keys, slamming doors, opening windows, or writing with a quill.

As part of this bonus action, the haunt can hurl the object up to 30 feet in any direction or use it as a ranged weapon to attack one creature within 30 feet of the object. The object has a +8 bonus to hit and deals 15 (2d10 + 4) bludgeoning damage on a hit. The GM might rule that a specific object deals piercing or slashing damage based on its form.

Creatures.0z288azn.Name: Kobold Master Inventor

Traits.Name: Cunning Action
Traits.Content: The kobold can take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action as a bonus action on each of its turns.

Traits.Name: Tactical Cowardice
Traits.Content: When a creature ends its turn within 5 feet of the kobold, the kobold may move up to 10 feet without provoking opportunity attacks.

Actions.Name: Dart Launcher
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage.

Actions.Name: Weapon Invention
Actions.Content: The kobold uses one of the following inventions (roll a d12 or choose one). The kobold can use each invention no more than once per day by choice, after that it has to roll a d12 as it pulls out the inventions from their faulty bag of holding:

Actions.Name: 1. Scorpion on a Stick
Actions.Content: The kobold makes a melee attack with a scorpion tied to a pole. Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the end of its next turn. While poisoned this way, the target takes 7 (2d6) poison damage at the start of its next turn.

Actions.Name: 2. Pocket Sand
Actions.Content: The kobold releases sand in a 15-foot cone. Creatures in the area must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or be blinded until the end of their next turn. On a success, a creature has disadvantage on its next attack roll.

Actions.Name: 3. Oil Flask
Actions.Content: The kobold throws a grenade to a point within 20 feet, creating a 10-foot-radius slick of oil for 1 round. Creatures entering or starting their turn in the area must succeed on a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw or fall prone. Creatures covered in oil are flammable.

Actions.Name: 4. Ignition Charge
Actions.Content: The kobold throws an incendiary charge to a point or creature within 30 feet. Creatures in the target space take 14 (4d6) fire damage, Dexterity save for half. If oil is present, the damage becomes 17 (5d6), and the oil ignites, dealing 7 (2d6) fire damage to creatures in the area at the start of their next turn.

Actions.Name: 5. Faerie Grenade
Actions.Content: The kobold throws a faerie dust charge at a Medium or smaller creature within 20 feet. The target must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or be affected by the levitate spell until the end of its next turn (no concentration required).

Actions.Name: 6. Quicksand Launcher
Actions.Content: The kobold fires a canister at a creature within 20 feet. The target must succeed on a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw or be restrained until the end of its next turn as its feet sink into rapidly hardening sand. A restrained creature can use its action to make a DC 16 Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check to free itself early.

Actions.Name: 7. Fork on a Plate
Actions.Content: The kobold scrapes a fork across a metal plate in a 15-foot radius. Creatures that can hear it must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or drop what they are holding, lose concentration, and have disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of their next turn. On a success, a creature has disadvantage on concentration checks until the Kobold Master Inventors next turn or until they stop scratching the plate.

Actions.Name: 8. Firework Shockwave
Actions.Content: The kobold detonates a cluster of fireworks in a 15-foot cube. Creatures in the area must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or take 14 (4d6) thunder damage, be pushed 10 feet, and be deafened until the end of their next turn. Half damage and no push on a success.

Actions.Name: 9. High-Explosive Grenade.
Actions.Content: The kobold throws a grenade to a point within 20 feet. Creatures in a 10-foot radius must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw or take 17 (5d6) force damage and be knocked prone. Half damage on a success, no prone.

Actions.Name: 10. Skunk in a Cage
Actions.Content: The kobold throws a caged skunk at a creature within 20 feet which releasing an overwhelming stench on the target. The Creature must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or begin retching violently and be incapacitated for 1 minute. An incapacitated creature may repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

Actions.Name: 11. Dragon Roar Amplifier
Actions.Content: The kobold unleashes an amplified roar in a 30-foot cone. Creatures in the area must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or be frightened of the kobold until the end of their next turn. Creatures already frightened instead take 7 (2d6) psychic damage.

Actions.Name: 12. A literal Snake
Actions.Content: The Kobold throws a rattlesnake at the target which makes a weapon attack (+7 to hit). On a hit the target takes 2 piercing damage and must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution Saving Throw or be Paralyzed until the Kobolds next turn.

BonusActions.Name: Smoke Bomb Step (3/day)
BonusActions.Content: The kobold throws a smoke charge at its feet and immediately teleports up to 20 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. The space it leaves is lightly obscured until the start of its next turn, and the kobold may take the Hide action as part of this effect.

Creatures.0z288azn.Description: A dangerously clever kobold inventor who replaces brute strength with battlefield control, volatile gadgets, and unfair tactics.

Creatures.19y7rt1d.Name: Jermlaine

Traits.Name: Lurkers in Shadow
Traits.Content: Jermlaine are invisible to darkvision.

Traits.Name: Magic Resistance
Traits.Content: The jermlaine has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Traits.Name: Vermin Friend
Traits.Content: The jermlaine can speak to and understand rats and giant rats.

Traits.Name: Swarming Rush
Traits.Content: A jermlaine adds 1d4 to its weapon damage rolls while within 5 feet of another, conscious jermlaine.

Actions.Name: Club
Actions.Content: Melee weapon attack: −1 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 1 (1d4 − 3) bludgeoning damage.

Actions.Name: Dart
Actions.Content: Ranged weapon attack: +3 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 3 (1d4 + 1) piercing damage.

Creatures.19y7rt1d.Description:
Jermlaine prove that terrible things can come in tiny packages. Their tiny, foot-tall forms can barely contain the cruelty and malevolence that courses through them. Deep in the Underdark, the jermlaine lurk among the tunnels, dungeons, and caverns inhabited by much larger, more dangerous folk. The jermlaine huddle in their small tunnels, avoiding contact with all other creatures unless they can marshal overwhelming numbers. When the odds are in their favor, they strike with a vicious determination that can overwhelm even a heavily armed drow patrol or duergar war party.

**Hateful Ambushers**. Jermlaine hate and resent all larger creatures. They are acutely aware that their small size and weakness in combat makes them prey for countless Underdark creatures. This anger and resentment is channeled into vengeful scheming against creatures that enter their domain. The jermlaine keep careful watch, waiting for just the right moment to fall upon their enemies. When they strike, they emerge from their tunnels to attack in overwhelming waves. In the heat of battle, individual jermlaine gladly throw themselves on their enemies’ weapons to clear the way for their allies’ attacks.

**Hidden Threat**. The jermlaine’s skin is rough, leathery, and textured like stone. Whether by the design of some sympathetic god or through sheer luck, their hide makes them appear invisible to darkvision. Part of the jermlaine’s anger comes from attempts to hunt them down and fashion cloaks and other material from their skin. Such experiments have yet to yield a functional magic item, and the jermlaine’s hatred of outsiders is driven in part by their assumption that any who meet them seek to harvest them for such experiments.

**Secret Lairs**. Because they are hunted for food or for their skin, jermlaine take care to hide their lairs away from prying eyes. They dig tunnels barely large enough to fit their 1-foot tall frames, making it almost impossible for their enemies to pursue them. Jermlaine dig their tunnels along dungeon hallways and Underdark passages, hiding the entrances to keep intruders out and set up their ambushes. The jermlaine create passages in the floor, along the walls, and in the upper part of the ceiling. When they attack, they spill upon their enemies from every direction in a bid to overwhelm them with sheer numbers.

**Allied Rodents**. The jermlaine can decipher the chitters and squeaks of rats, and in turn can communicate with such rodents. Underdark explorers know to keep a careful watch when encountering signs of a rat infestation. Those rodents, as creatures of the surface world, are rare in the deep Underdark. Their presence invariably signals the presence of a jermlaine colony.

Traits.Name: Brute
Traits.Content: A melee weapon deals one extra die of its damage when the bugbear hits with it (included in the attack).

Traits.Name: Surprise Attack
Traits.Content: If the bugbear surprises a creature and hits it with an attack during the first round of combat, the target takes an extra 7 (2d6) damage from the attack.

Actions.Name: Morningstar
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d8 + 2) piercing damage.

Actions.Name: Javelin
Actions.Content: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (2d6 + 2) piercing damage in melee or 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage at range.

Actions.Name: Multi-attack
Actions.Content: Tovrak makes two attacks with his morningstar or javelin.

LegendaryActions.Name: Rain of rats
LegendaryActions.Content:
At the start of Tovraks turn he yells "FEEDING TIME!" and bangs his morningstar in the floor/walls. You hear a lot of skittering, like a hundred small feet.

4 Swarm of Rats appears through cracks in the walls and dropping down through the ceiling. The swarms gets the same initiative as Tovrak.

Traits.Name: Amphibious
Traits.Content: The isopod can breathe air and water.

Traits.Name: Stone Camouflage
Traits.Content: The isopod has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks to hide in rocky terrain.

Actions.Name: Bite
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) piercing damage.

Actions.Name: Seize
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one Medium or smaller creature. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) bludgeoning damage, and the target is grappled (escape DC 14). Until this grapple ends, the isopod can’t make a Seize attack against another target.

Reactions.Name: Curl
Reactions.Content: When a creature within 5 feet of the isopod hits them with an attack, the isopod defensively rolls up inside their rocky carapace. The isopod gains a +2 bonus to AC against the triggering attack, and the attacker must succeed on a DC 14 Strength saving throw or take 4 (1d8) bludgeoning damage and be pushed 5 feet into an unoccupied space of the isopod’s choice.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The goblin monstrosity makes three attack with its arms. One of them is with the Dagger of Silent Screams.

Actions.Name: Dagger of Silent Screams (BONUS A)
Actions.Content:
If a creature is hit with this dagger the monstrosity can use it's bonus action to try and steal it's voice. DC 15 INT, if successfull the target is immune to the effect for one hour.

If failed, they lose their voice for one hour and is HEALED for 2d6 but experience is as Psychic damage and is stunned for one round.

Actions.Name: Torture tools (+5 to hit)
Actions.Content: The monstrosity attacks with the torture tools it has in its hands dealing 7 (1d6+3) slashing damage.

Reactions.Name: +5 to hit Throw Dagger of Silent Screams
Reactions.Content:
As a reaction to a vocal spell being cast the monstrosity can throw its dagger at the target. If it hits, the spell is lost as well as the targets voice, they are stunned for one round but they get 2d6 healing.

Note that the dagger has to be retrieved before being able to use the reaction again.

LegendaryActions.Name: Reel them in (+5 to hit) (40ft range)
LegendaryActions.Content:
When the combat starts the monstrosity will throw a hook and a chain with its fourth arm (+5 to hit). If it connects the target takes 5 piercing damage and is considered Grappled.

The monstrosity will then make a CONTESTED STR check and if it wins it will yank the target towards it so it ends up in melee.

Note that this action can be repeated at the beginning of each of its turns and can be done from the roof.

Creatures.1f8g3x9q.Name: Swamp Dryad
DamageResistances: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from mundane attacks
Languages: any languages the dryad spoke before their binding

Traits.Name: Supernatural Resistance
Traits.Content: The dryad has advantage on saving throws against supernatural effects.

Traits.Name: Speak with Beasts and Plants
Traits.Content: The dryad can communicate with Beasts and Plants as if they shared a language.

Traits.Name: Tree Stride (1/Turn)
Traits.Content: The dryad can use 10 feet of movement to step into one living tree within their reach and emerge from a second living tree within 60 feet of the first tree, appearing in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the second tree. Both trees must be Large or larger.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The dryad makes two Slam attacks and uses Entangling Roots, if available.

Actions.Name: Slam
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) piercing damage, and the target must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 14 (4d6) poison damage and is poisoned for 1 minute. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage and isn’t poisoned.

Actions.Name: Entangling Roots (Recharge 5–6)
Actions.Content:
The dryad chooses a point they can see within 60 feet of them, causing tree roots to pull free of the ground and attack in a 15-foot radius centered on that point. If the dryad is within 5 feet of their tree, the radius of this effect doubles to 30 feet. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 18 Strength saving throw or take 22 (4d10) bludgeoning damage and be grappled (escape DC 14). While grappled in this way, a creature is restrained and their flesh begins to harden and turn to wood.

At the end of a creature’s third consecutive turn being grappled in this way, they become petrified and turn into a hardwood statue. The petrified condition ends for that creature if the dryad uses an action to end it, or if the dryad dies.

Actions.Name: Fey Intimidation
Actions.Content: The dryad targets up to five creatures they can see within 30 feet of them. Each target who can see the dryad must succeed on a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw or be frightened of the dryad (save ends at end of turn). If a target succeeds on their saving throw or the effect ends for them, the target is immune to the swamp dryad’s Fey Intimidation for the next 24 hours.

BonusActions.Name: Swamp Medicine (3/Day)
BonusActions.Content: The dryad touches a creature, who regains 18 (3d8 + 5) hit points. Alternatively, the creature removes one disease or neutralizes one poison afflicting them.

Creatures.1i5f76pq.Name: Flintmoth Swarm
Creatures.1i5f76pq.Type: Huge Swarm of Tiny Elementals, Unaligned

Traits.Name: Obscuring Form
Traits.Content: Other creatures in the swarm’s space are blinded.

Traits.Name: Shrink
Traits.Content: When the swarm is reduced to half their hit points, their size becomes Large.

Traits.Name: Stone Camouflage
Traits.Content: The swarm has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide in rocky terrain.

Traits.Name: Swarm
Traits.Content: The swarm can occupy another creature’s space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny insect. The swarm can’t regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The swarm makes three Flutter Cutter attacks.

Actions.Name: Flutter Cutter
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 0 ft., one target in the swarm’s space. Hit: 26 (6d6 + 5) slashing damage, or 33 (8d6 + 5) slashing damage if the target is a Construct made of stone or is holding or wearing an object that emits bright light.

BonusActions.Name: Devour Stone
BonusActions.Content: The swarm feeds on a mundane object or structure within 5 feet of them that is made mostly of stone. If the swarm targets a Medium or smaller object, it is entirely devoured. If the swarm targets a structure or a Large or larger object, the swarm devours a 5-foot-cube portion of it.

Creatures.1jrkyqx8.Name: Sandstrider (Jungle Phantom)

Traits.Name: Multiattack
Traits.Content: The Jungle Phantom makes 3 Clawed Gauntlet attack whenever they take the attack action.

Traits.Name: Whisperclaw Ambush (3/day)
Traits.Content: As a Bonus Action while hidden or attacking from above, the Jungle Phantom gains advantage on its attacks, and if they hit, they deals an extra 1d6 damage and the Phantom may move up to 10 ft. without provoking opportunity attacks.

Traits.Name: Rootstep (3/day)
Traits.Content: As a Reaction when an attack misses it or an ally within 10 ft., the Phantom melds briefly into vegetation, takes the hide action and "teleports" 15ft.

Traits.Name: Phantom’s Veil (1/day)
Traits.Content: As an Action, the Phantom makes one Clawed Gauntlet attack and then becomes hard to see as copys of it show up (Lightbender ability) for 1 minute. It gains advantage on Stealth checks, attacks against it have disadvantage, and it can move through nonmagical plants without slowing. The effect ends early if exposed to bright light.

Actions.Name: Clawed Gauntlets
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8+3) slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Bola
Actions.Content:
Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8+3) bludgeoning damage and the target must make a Dexterity saving throw DC 14 or be restrained.

Removing the Bola from another target is an action.

Creatures.1jrkyqx8.Description: Stealthy Sandstriders adapted to the jungles of Vel’kihrm, moving through roots and vines like living shadows.

Traits.Name: Infernal Ichor
Traits.Content: When the incendiarist dies, their corpse unleashes a spray of burning orange ichor. Each creature within 5 feet of the incendiarist takes 3 fire damage.

Actions.Name: Shortsword
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) piercing damage.

Actions.Name: Blazing Crossbow
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, range 100/400 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) piercing damage, and if the target is a creature, they are set on fire. While on fire, the target takes 3 (1d6) fire damage at the end of each of their turns, and the target or another creature who can reach them can use an action to put out the flames. A creature who is already on fire suffers no additional effect from being set on fire in this way again.

Actions.Name: Fire Mote (Recharge 6)
Actions.Content: The incendiarist shoots an explosive mote of fire at a creature they can see within 100 feet of them. The target must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or take 7 (2d6) fire damage and, if they are Large or smaller, fall prone. All other creatures within 15 feet of the target must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or take 3 (1d6) fire damage.

Traits.Name: Detached Eyes
Traits.Content: Six eyes float around the overmind and create their Eye Psionics effects. Each eye is an object that has AC 24, 1 hit point, and a psionic shield that makes it immune to damage. After an eye creates a psionic effect, it loses its psionic shield until the start of the overmind’s next turn. If an eye is destroyed, a new eye pops out of the overmind’s face to replace it at the end of their next turn.

Actions.Name: Bite
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (4d6) piercing damage.

Actions.Name: Eye Psionics
Actions.Content:
The overmind creates three of the following psionic eye effects. Unless otherwise stated, each eye targets a creature who the overmind can see within 120 feet of them. They can’t use the same effect twice in a turn:

1. **Charm Beam.** The targeted creature must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by the overmind for 1 hour, or until the overmind harms the creature or one of their allies.
2. **Compulsion Beam.** The targeted creature must succeed on a DC 16 Intelligence saving throw or use their reaction, if available, to move up to their speed toward the closest ally they can see and make a weapon attack against them. Creatures who can’t be charmed are unaffected.
3. **Toxic Vapors.** The overmind chooses a point they can see within 120 feet of them. Each creature within 10 feet of the point must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn).
4. **Telekinetic Beam.** The targeted creature must succeed on a DC 16 Strength saving throw or be moved up to 30 feet in any direction and take 7 (2d6) force damage at the end of the move. Alternatively, the overmind can target an object they can see within 120 feet of them that weighs 300 pounds or less and isn’t being worn or carried, moving it up to 30 feet in any direction. The overmind can also exert fine control on objects with this effect, such as manipulating a simple tool or opening a door or a container.
5. **Lightning Bolt.** The overmind shoots a 5-foot-wide, 60-foot-long line of lightning. Each creature in the line must succeed on a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw, taking 21 (6d6) lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
6. **Fire Beam.** The targeted creature must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 36 (8d8) fire damage and catches fire, taking 9 (2d8) fire damage at the start of each of their turns for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn). Any creature can use their action to douse the fire, ending the effect early.

BonusActions.Name: The Great Eye (Recharge 6)
BonusActions.Content: The overmind’s central eye turns solid black and projects a 150-foot cone of energy. If the overmind or any creature in that area is affected by a spell, the spell’s effects immediately end for that creature.

Reactions.Name: Away with You!
Reactions.Content: When a creature within 5 feet of the overmind hits them with a melee attack, the overmind can use Telekinetic Beam on the attacker.

Creatures.1y4873h7.Name: Vivienn Dirroze
DamageResistances: necrotic (bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing during Draconic Rage)

Traits.Name: Exploit Opening (3/Day)
Traits.Content: When Vivienn makes an attack, she has advantage on the attack roll.

Traits.Name: Reckless
Traits.Content: At the start of her turn, Vivienn can gain advantage on all melee weapon attack rolls made during her turn, but attack rolls made against her have advantage until the start of her next turn.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: Vivienn makes three Godslayer Axe attacks.

Actions.Name: Godslayer Axe
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (1d12 + 8) slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) necrotic damage. If the target is a Celestial, they take an extra 18 (4d8) necrotic damage.

BonusActions.Name: Draconic Rage (3/Day)
BonusActions.Content:
Vivienn roars with the might of dragons, entering a rage that lasts for 1 minute or until she is incapacitated. While raging, she gains the following benefits:
• Her size becomes Large.
• She has a +2 bonus to weapon damage rolls.
• She has advantage on Strength ability checks and saving throws.
• She has resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

BonusActions.Name: Ravenous Bite (Draconic Rage Only)
BonusActions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (1d12 + 8) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 19 Constitution saving throw or be frightened of Vivienn for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn).

Creatures.20183jub.Name: Iarno "Glasstaff" Albrak

Traits.Name: Glass sculpture
Traits.Content:
When the first melee attack is made versus Glasstaff his body shatters into thousands pieces of glass and he takes no damage from the attack. Every creature within 10ft of Glasstaff needs to make a DEX saving throw DC 13 and take 6 damage on a failed save and half as much on a successful save.

Glasstaff then immediately reforms up to 30 ft. away from his previous position.

A wide area of broken glass appears between the area Glasstaff teleported from and where he arrived (15 feet long, full room width) and this is considered tough terrain.

Traits.Name: Mirror image
Traits.Content: In the room there are 3 mirrors. One mirror shatters when a mirror image is destroyed, likewise a mirror image is destroyed if a mirror is shattered.

Actions.Name: Spellcasting
Actions.Content:
Cantrips (at will): Firebolt, Shocking grasp
1st level (4 slots): Mirror image, Magic missile
2nd level (3 slots): Hold person, Misty step

Actions.Name: Quarterstaff
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +1 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d8) bludgeoning damage.

LegendaryActions.Name: Counterspell
LegendaryActions.Content:
Costs 1 legendary action.

Casting Time: 1 legendary action, which you take when you see a creature within 60 feet of you casting a spell
Range: 60 feet
Components: S
Duration: Instantaneous

You attempt to interrupt a creature in the process of casting a spell. If the creature is casting a spell of 3rd level or lower, its spell fails and has no effect. If it is casting a spell of 4th level or higher, make an ability check using your spellcasting ability. The DC equals 10 + the spell’s level. On a success, the creature’s spell fails and has no effect.

LegendaryActions.Name: Shield
LegendaryActions.Content:
Costs 1 legendary action.

An invisible barrier of magical force appears and protects you. Until the start of your next turn, you have a +5 bonus to AC, including against the triggering attack, and you take no damage from magic missile.

Creatures.20183jub.Description: A former member of the Lords' Alliance, larno seized an opportunity in Phandalin to line his own pockets. Originally tasked with setting up a constabulary, the mage instead assembled a group of outlaws and local ruffians to secure his own position in town.

Traits.Name: Stubborn Rage
Traits.Content: The wyvern is immune to the frightened and stunned conditions while they have fewer than half their hit points or are within 60 feet of another wyvern they can hear or see.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The wyvern makes one Stinger attack and one Bite attack. They can replace one attack with a use of Tail Sweep.

Actions.Name: Stinger
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (3d6 + 3) piercing damage. The target must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or take 7 (2d6) acid damage at the start of each of their turns for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn). A cure ailment power or a lesser restoration spell ends this effect on a target.

Actions.Name: Bite
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 16 (2d12 + 3) piercing damage. If the target is Large or smaller, they are grappled (escape DC 13). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained and the wyvern can’t bite another target.

Actions.Name: Tail Sweep
Actions.Content: The wyvern swipes their tail in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 14 Strength saving throw or take 14 (2d10 + 3) bludgeoning damage and be pushed up to 10 feet away from the wyvern.

BonusActions.Name: Seek
BonusActions.Content: The wyvern takes the Search action.

Traits.Name: Infernal Ichor
Traits.Content: When Varrox dies, his corpse unleashes a spray of burning orange ichor. Each creature within 5 feet of him takes 5 fire damage.

Traits.Name: Infernal Sacrifice (3/Day)
Traits.Content: When Varrox fails a saving throw, he can call on Hell to succeed instead. When he does, each ally within 60 feet of him takes 5 necrotic damage.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: Varrox makes three attacks using Soulsword, Death Skulls, or both.

Actions.Name: Soulsword
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) slashing damage plus 10 (3d6) necrotic damage. If the target is a creature, they can’t regain hit points until the start of Varrox’s next turn.

Actions.Name: Death Skulls
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) piercing damage plus 9 (2d8) fire damage, and Varrox can teleport to an unoccupied space he can see within 5 feet of the target.

BonusActions.Name: Advance and Attack
BonusActions.Content: Varrox barks orders at one ally within 60 feet of him who can hear him. That ally can use their reaction to move up to their speed and make a weapon attack.

Reactions.Name: An Army from Blood
Reactions.Content: When a non-minion hobgoblin who Varrox can see within 60 feet of him takes damage, Varrox magically summons three hobgoblin recruits who appear in unoccupied spaces nearest to the damaged hobgoblin.

MythicActions.Name: Uncanny Leadership
MythicActions.Content: Varrox chooses up to six non-minion allies within 60 feet of him who can see or hear him. Varrox and each chosen creature gain 10 temporary hit points and can move up to their speed.

MythicActions.Name: Hellish Transport
MythicActions.Content: Varrox and each ally within 60 feet of him can teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space they can see.

MythicActions.Name: I Am Fire and Death
MythicActions.Content: Each enemy within 20 feet of Varrox must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw, taking 11 (2d10) fire damage plus 11 (2d10) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. After the explosion, a shroud of black flame covers Varrox for 1 minute. For the duration, each time another creature hits Varrox with an attack while within 5 feet of him or touches him, that creature takes 5 (1d10) fire damage plus 5 (1d10) necrotic damage.

Creatures.28yp4h7m.Name: Mummy
Languages: the languages they knew in life

Traits.Name: Flammable
Traits.Content: Whenever the mummy takes fire damage, they take an extra 5 (1d10) fire damage.

Traits.Name: Mummy Dust
Traits.Content: Whenever the mummy takes piercing or slashing damage, each creature within 5 feet of them takes 4 (1d8) poison damage.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The mummy makes two Desiccating Slam attacks and uses Guardian Curse.

Actions.Name: Desiccating Slam
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) bludgeoning damage. Until the start of the mummy’s next turn, the target’s speed is reduced by 10 feet and the target can’t regain hit points.

Actions.Name: Guardian Curse
Actions.Content: The mummy calls down a scourge on one creature they can see within 60 feet of them. The target must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or be cursed. While cursed in this way, whenever the target takes damage, they take an extra 3 (1d6) necrotic damage that can’t be reduced in any way. The curse lasts until removed by a cure ailment power, a remove curse spell, or a similar supernatural effect. A target who succeeds on their saving throw is immune to the Guardian Curse of all mummies for the next 24 hours.

Traits.Name: Alchemical Ingredients
Traits.Content: After the basilisk dies, a creature can make a DC 12 Wisdom (Medicine) check using an herbalism kit and the basilisk’s gullet, destroying the gullet in the process. On a success, the creature creates three doses of a salve. One dose of this salve can be applied to a petrified creature as an action, and 1 minute after the salve is applied, the petrified condition ends for that creature.

Traits.Name: Toxic Ichor
Traits.Content: A creature who deals piercing or slashing damage to the basilisk while within 5 feet of them takes 2 (1d4) poison damage.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The basilisk uses Petrifying Eye Beams and makes one Bite attack or uses Poison Fumes, if available.

Actions.Name: Bite
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) piercing damage plus 5 (2d4) poison damage.

Actions.Name: Petrifying Eye Beams
Actions.Content: The basilisk targets one or two creatures they can see within 30 feet of them. Each target must succeed on a DC 12 Constitution saving throw or be restrained as they magically begin to turn to stone. Until the end of the restrained creature’s next turn, they or an ally within 5 feet of them can use an action to cut the encroaching stone from the restrained creature’s body using a weapon that deals slashing damage. When they do, the restrained creature takes 13 (2d12) slashing damage, which can’t be reduced in any way, and they are no longer restrained. A creature restrained in this way must repeat the saving throw at the end of their turn. On a successful save, the effect ends. On a failed save, this creature is petrified until freed by a cure ailment power of 4th order or higher, a greater restoration spell, or a similar supernatural effect.

Actions.Name: Poison Fumes (Recharge 4–6)
Actions.Content: The basilisk exhales poisonous fumes in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in this area must make a DC 12 Constitution saving throw, taking 10 (4d4) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Reactions.Name: Poison Splash
Reactions.Content: When the basilisk takes piercing or slashing damage, their wound spews poison. Each creature within 10 feet of the basilisk must succeed on a DC 12 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn).

Creatures.2j8myyp1.Name: Ashyra
DamageResistances: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from mundane attacks

Traits.Name: Atonement (3/Day)
Traits.Content: If Ashyra fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead. When she does, she must either end the effect of her Ancient Curse on one enemy within 120 feet of her or be dazed until the end of her next turn.

Traits.Name: Mummy Dust
Traits.Content: Whenever Ashyra takes piercing or slashing damage, each creature within 5 feet of her takes 9 (2d8) poison damage.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: Ashyra makes two attacks using Desiccating Slam, Soul Fire, or both, and she uses Ancient Curse.

Actions.Name: Desiccating Slam
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (3d6 + 4) bludgeoning damage plus 10 (3d6) necrotic damage. The target’s speed is reduced to 0, and they can’t regain hit points until the start of Ashyra’s next turn.

Actions.Name: Soul Fire (3rd-Level Spell)
Actions.Content: Ranged Spell Attack: +9 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 20 (3d10 + 4) radiant damage, and if the target is a creature, their hit point maximum decreases by a number equal to the damage taken. This reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest or is targeted by a cure ailment power of 4th order or higher, a greater restoration spell, or a similar supernatural effect. The target dies if this effect reduces their hit point maximum to 0.

Actions.Name: Ancient Curse
Actions.Content: Ashyra inflicts weakness on one creature she can see within 120 feet of her. The target must succeed on a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw or be cursed. While the target is cursed in this way, their attacks deal half damage. The curse lasts until Ashyra chooses to end it or until removed by a cure ailment power, a remove curse spell, or a similar supernatural effect.

BonusActions.Name: Misty Step (2nd-Level Spell)
BonusActions.Content: Ashyra teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space she can see.

Reactions.Name: Weight of Ages
Reactions.Content: When a creature Ashyra can see within 60 feet of her deals damage to her, Ashyra brings down the weight of ages on them. The creature must succeed on a DC 17 Strength saving throw or be restrained until the end of their next turn.

MythicActions.Name: Sands of the Ancients
MythicActions.Content: A storm of stinging dirt whirls around Ashyra in a 15-foot-radius until the end of her next turn. For the duration, that area is heavily obscured, and when a creature starts their turn in or enters that area for the first time on a turn, they must succeed on a DC 17 Constitution saving throw or take 22 (4d10) necrotic damage.

MythicActions.Name: Dust to Dust
MythicActions.Content: Ashyra bursts into a cloud of dust and makes one Desiccating Slam attack against each enemy within 60 feet of her, then she reforms in an unoccupied space within 60 feet of her original location.

MythicActions.Name: Last Rites
MythicActions.Content: Ashyra commands the land to entomb her foes. Each enemy within 30 feet of her must make a DC 17 Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 21 (6d6) bludgeoning damage and is entombed in stone. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage and is not entombed. An entombed creature is restrained, has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the stone, and takes 7 (2d6) necrotic damage at the end of each of their turns. The creature can be freed if the stone is destroyed (AC 17; 30 hit points; immunity to poison and psychic damage).

Creatures.2uape0rp.Name: Pitling
Creatures.2uape0rp.Type: Tiny Fiend (Demon, Category 1), Typically Chaotic Evil

Traits.Name: Horrid Stench
Traits.Content: If an enemy starts their turn within 10 feet of three or more pitlings, the enemy is poisoned until the start of their next turn.

Traits.Name: Minion
Traits.Content: If the pitling takes damage from an attack or as the result of a failed saving throw, their hit points are reduced to 0. If the pitling takes damage from another effect, they die if the damage equals or exceeds their hit point maximum; otherwise they take no damage.

Traits.Name: Soul Feast
Traits.Content: When the pitling reduces a creature who isn’t a Construct or an Undead to 0 hit points or deals damage to a dying creature, the creature must make a DC 11 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the pitling consumes the creature’s soul, and the pitling becomes a Category 2 demon of the GM’s choice with 1 soul. A creature whose soul is consumed in this way immediately dies, and they can’t be restored to life by any means short of a wish spell. If the creature was reduced to 0 hit points by a group attack, the GM picks one pitling who joined the attack to consume the soul.

Actions.Name: Spit (Group Attack)
Actions.Content: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 15/30 ft., one target. Hit: 4 poison damage.

Traits.Name: Shield? Shield!
Traits.Content: The signifer gains a +1 bonus to AC while within 5 feet of at least one ally who also has this trait, is wielding a shield, and isn’t incapacitated or prone. To benefit from this trait, the signifer must be wielding a shield.

Traits.Name: Standard Bearer
Traits.Content: Each kobold unit can have one or more signifers, whose weapon is their unit’s battle standard. If a signifer drops their signum, they use the kobold tiro stat block instead. If an allied kobold tiro uses an action to pick up a signum not carried by another creature, that tiro uses the kobold signifer stat block instead. When a signifer or tiro’s stat block changes in this way, any conditions or effects currently affecting them remain active, and their hit points and hit point maximum remain the same. A signum that isn’t being held by a creature can be damaged; it has AC 15, 5 hit points, and immunity to poison and psychic damage.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The signifer makes two Signum attacks.

Actions.Name: Signum
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) bludgeoning damage.

Actions.Name: Shield Wall Formation
Actions.Content: The signifer chooses up to four kobolds within 60 feet of them who can hear them. Each kobold can use their reaction to move up to their speed, and each kobold who does so must end this movement within 5 feet of an ally.

BonusActions.Name: Glory to the Legion
BonusActions.Content: (1/Day). The signifer and each ally within 30 feet of them who can hear them regains 5 (2d4) hit points.

Traits.Name: Tunneler
Traits.Content: The drangolin can burrow through solid rock at half their burrow speed and leaves behind a 3-foot-diameter tunnel when they burrow. Tiny and Small creatures can move through it normally, and Medium and Large creatures can squeeze through it. This tunnel collapses after 10 minutes.

Actions.Name: Fiery Claws
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) fire damage.

Actions.Name: Erupt (1/Day)
Actions.Content: If the drangolin is burrowing within 10 feet of the surface, they can erupt from the ground (without spending movement) into a space that contains one or more creatures. When they do, each creature within 10 feet of the drangolin must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw. A creature directly above the drangolin when they emerge has disadvantage on this saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 14 (4d6) bludgeoning damage and is knocked prone. On a successful save, a creature takes only half as much damage and is pushed 5 feet to an unoccupied space of their choice instead of being knocked prone.

Actions.Name: Wrecking Ball (1/Day)
Actions.Content: The drangolin moves up to twice their walking speed in a straight line without provoking opportunity attacks. While doing so, they can enter Large or smaller creatures’ spaces. If the drangolin enters another creature’s space during this move, that creature must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or take 7 (2d6) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) fire damage. If the drangolin enters the space of a structure or object that isn’t being worn or carried, it takes twice as much damage.

Reactions.Name: Ash Shot
Reactions.Content: When a creature the drangolin can sense moves within 5 feet of them, the drangolin sprays them with a cloud of ash. The target must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or be blinded until the start of their next turn.

Creatures.3hhqvx0b.Name: Multivok Chief
DamageResistances: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from mundane attacks

Traits.Name: Immutable Form
Traits.Content: The chief is immune to any power, spell, or effect that would alter their form.

Traits.Name: Supernatural Resistance
Traits.Content: The chief has advantage on saving throws against powers, spells, and other supernatural effects.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The chief makes three attacks using Pneumatic Fist, Targeting Beam, or both.

Actions.Name: Pneumatic Fist
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 21 (3d10 + 5) bludgeoning damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, the chief can push them up to 10 feet away.

Actions.Name: Targeting Beam
Actions.Content: Ranged Spell Attack: +10 to hit, range 150 ft., one target. Hit: 26 (4d12) force damage, and the target is marked by a beam of red light until the end of the chief’s next turn. While the target is marked, attack rolls against them have advantage.

BonusActions.Name: Chief’s Command
BonusActions.Content: The chief shouts a command to an ally within 60 feet of them who can hear them. The ally can either move up to their speed without provoking opportunity attacks or make a melee attack (no action required).

Traits.Name: Death Snap
Traits.Content: When the hyena is reduced to 0 hit points, they can deal 1 piercing damage to a creature within 5 feet of them (no action required), provided they weren’t incapacitated before dropping to 0 hit points.

Traits.Name: Minion
Traits.Content: If the hyena takes damage from an attack or as the result of a failed saving throw, their hit points are reduced to 0. If the hyena takes damage from another effect, they die if the damage equals or exceeds their hit point maximum; otherwise they take no damage.

Actions.Name: Bite (Group Attack)
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 1 piercing damage. If the target is a creature and two or more hyenas joined the attack, the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. The DC for this saving throw equals 10 plus the number of hyenas who joined the attack.

Creatures.3meadrq7.Name: Kraken
DamageImmunities: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from mundane attacks
Languages: understands all languages but can’t speak

Traits.Name: Amphibious
Traits.Content: The Kraken can breathe air and water.

Traits.Name: Discorporation
Traits.Content: When the Kraken drops to 0 hit points or dies, all conditions and other effects end for them. They teleport back to the place where they slumber, return to life with all their hit points, and fall unconscious.

Traits.Name: Siege Monster
Traits.Content: The Kraken deals double damage to objects and structures.

Traits.Name: Slowed Resistance (3/Day)
Traits.Content: If the Kraken fails a saving throw, they can choose to succeed instead, and their speed is reduced by a cumulative 10 feet for 24 hours.

Traits.Name: Supernatural Resistance
Traits.Content: The Kraken has advantage on saving throws against powers, spells, and other supernatural effects.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The Kraken makes four Tentacle attacks or uses Mental Lance three times.

Actions.Name: Tentacle
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +16 to hit, reach 30 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (2d10 + 8) bludgeoning damage plus 11 (2d10) psychic damage, and the target is grappled (escape DC 18). The Kraken has ten tentacles, each of which can grapple one target.

Actions.Name: Mental Lance (3rd-Order Power)
Actions.Content: Ranged Power Attack: +18 to hit, range 120 ft., one creature. Hit: 22 (4d10) psychic damage, and the target is frightened of the Kraken until the start of the Kraken’s next turn.

Actions.Name: Psionic Storm
Actions.Content: The Kraken creates a psionic storm, entrapping a vehicle they can see within 120 feet of them. While entrapped in the storm, the vehicle is restrained. The storm ends if the Kraken’s concentration is broken or if they are more than 120 feet from the vehicle. If the Kraken’s turn ends three times before the storm ends, the vehicle is reduced to 0 hit points.

Actions.Name: Skull-Splitting Howl (1/Day)
Actions.Content: The Kraken telepathically howls in the mind of each enemy within 120 feet of them. Each target must make a DC 26 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 55 (10d10) psychic damage and can’t concentrate on powers or spells until the start of the Kraken’s next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage and suffers no other effect.

BonusActions.Name: Telekinetic Fling
BonusActions.Content: The Kraken throws a creature they are grappling into an unoccupied space the Kraken can see within 60 feet of them. The target lands prone and takes 22 (4d10) bludgeoning damage.

Reactions.Name: Psychic Reprisal
Reactions.Content: When the Kraken is damaged by an attack, each enemy within 30 feet of the attacker (including the attacker) must make a DC 26 Wisdom saving throw, taking 22 (4d10) psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

MythicActions.Name: Psionic Tentacles
MythicActions.Content: Psionically conjured tentacles make a Tentacle attack on each enemy within 120 feet of the Kraken. The Kraken then uses Telekinetic Fling on each creature they are grappling.

MythicActions.Name: Psionic Ink
MythicActions.Content: The Kraken teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space they can see. Each creature within 30 feet of the space the Kraken left must make a DC 26 Dexterity saving throw or be covered in psionic ink, which they can use an action to wipe away. A creature covered in psionic ink takes 16 (3d10) psychic damage at the start of each of their turns.

MythicActions.Name: The Ocean’s Wrath
MythicActions.Content: The Kraken unleashes a wave of intense psionic power. Each creature within 120 feet of the Kraken must make a DC 26 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 65 (10d12) psychic damage and is frightened of the Kraken for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn). On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn’t frightened.

Traits.Name: Exceptional Vision
Traits.Content: The bird has a +10 bonus to Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.

Traits.Name: Flyby
Traits.Content: The bird doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks when they fly out of an enemy’s reach.

Traits.Name: High-Speed Dive (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
Traits.Content: If the bird descends at least 30 feet on their turn, they can take the Dash action as a bonus action. When they do, they have advantage on the next attack they make before the end of their turn.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The bird makes two attacks using Heavy Beak, Rending Talons, or both.

Actions.Name: Heavy Beak
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage. On a critical hit, the target is dazed until the start of the bird’s next turn.

Actions.Name: Rending Talons
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) slashing damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, they are grappled (escape DC 13). While grappling a target, the bird’s Beak attacks against that target are made with advantage, and the bird can’t attack another target. Each time the target makes a check to end the grapple, the target takes 4 (1d8) slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Crushing Grip
Actions.Content: The bird’s powerful talons crush a grappled target. The target must make a DC 13 Strength saving throw, taking 17 (5d6) bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. If the target fails the save by 5 or more, they are also dazed until the end of their next turn.

BonusActions.Name: Spot
BonusActions.Content: The bird makes a Wisdom (Perception) check.

Traits.Name: Charm Resistant
Traits.Content: Lestheris has advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the charmed condition on herself.

Traits.Name: Dark Calling
Traits.Content: Each other creature within 30 feet of Lestheris has disadvantage on death saving throws.

Traits.Name: Tome of the Ethereal
Traits.Content: Lestheris can see 120 feet into the Ethereal Plane.

Traits.Name: Utility Spells
Traits.Content:
In addition to any other spells in this stat block, Lestheris can cast the following spells, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14):

- **At will:** mage hand, message, minor illusion
- **3/day each:** detect magic, mage armor
- **1/day each:** knock, speak with dead

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: Lestheris makes two Grave Grasp or two Necrotic Lance attacks.

Actions.Name: Grave Grasp (Cantrip)
Actions.Content: Melee Spell Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (2d6) necrotic damage, and the target can’t regain hit points until the start of Lestheris’s next turn.

Actions.Name: Necrotic Lance (1st-Level Spell)
Actions.Content: Ranged Spell Attack: +6 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (2d8) necrotic damage. Lestheris can destroy a shade under her control within 30 feet of her and deal an extra 4 (1d8) necrotic damage to the target.

Actions.Name: Animate Spirits (2/Day)
Actions.Content: Lestheris animates two shades. The shades appear in unoccupied spaces Lestheris can see within 30 feet of her. The shades act immediately after Lestheris on the same initiative count and follow her verbal orders. If Lestheris dies, the shades are destroyed.

Reactions.Name: Ethereal Step (3/Day)
Reactions.Content:
When a creature Lestheris can see within 60 feet of her targets her with an attack, power, or spell while she isn’t on the Ethereal Plane, she can choose one of the following effects:

- Lestheris enters the Ethereal Plane, then returns to the plane and the space she left at the start of her next turn. If that space is occupied, she returns to the nearest unoccupied space of her choice.
- She teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space she can see.

Creatures.3xh499bk.Name: High Mage Vairae
DamageResistances: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from mundane attacks

Traits.Name: Draining Resistance (3/Day)
Traits.Content: If Vairae fails a saving throw, they can choose to succeed instead. When they do, Vairae has disadvantage on spell attacks until the end of their next turn.

Traits.Name: Rejuvenation
Traits.Content: Vairae has the Rejuvenation trait found in the lich stat block.

Traits.Name: Supernatural Resistance
Traits.Content: Vairae has advantage on saving throws against powers, spells, and other supernatural effects.

Traits.Name: Turn Immunity
Traits.Content: Vairae is immune to effects that turn Undead.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: Vairae uses Glare of Undeath and makes four attacks using Conflagration, Immortal Pain, Magic Drain, or a combination of them.

Actions.Name: Conflagration (4th-Level Spell)
Actions.Content: Ranged Spell Attack: +14 to hit, range 150 ft., one target. Hit: 35 (10d6) fire damage, and if the target takes both an action and a bonus action on their next turn, they take an extra 17 (5d6) fire damage at the end of that turn.

Actions.Name: Immortal Pain (4th-Level Spell)
Actions.Content: Melee or Ranged Spell Attack: +14 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 90 ft., one creature. Hit: 28 (8d6) psychic damage, and the target is afflicted with pain. While afflicted, the creature’s attacks deal half damage. After each attack, the afflicted creature can make a DC 22 Constitution saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

Actions.Name: Magic Drain (4th-Level Spell)
Actions.Content: Melee or Ranged Spell Attack: +14 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., one creature. Hit: 26 (4d12) necrotic damage. If the target is currently affected by any spells not cast by Vairae, they must succeed on a DC 22 Wisdom saving throw or those effects end for the target.

Actions.Name: Glare of Undeath
Actions.Content: Vairae glares at one creature they can see within 60 feet. The target must succeed on a DC 22 Wisdom saving throw or be restrained for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn). A creature the target can hear and understand can use an action to make a DC 22 Charisma (Persuasion) check, ending the effect on a success.

Actions.Name: Machinations of Bone (2/Day)
Actions.Content: Each enemy within 30 feet of a point Vairae can see within 150 feet must make a DC 22 Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 54 (12d8) piercing damage, falls prone, and can’t stand up until they receive supernatural healing.

BonusActions.Name: Necrotic Form (3rd-Level Spell)
BonusActions.Content: Vairae enters a shadow form and can move up to their speed. While in this form, Vairae is immune to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage and the grappled and restrained conditions. Vairae reverts to their true form at the end of their turn.

Reactions.Name: Baleful Teleport (6th-Level Spell)
Reactions.Content: When a creature targets Vairae with an attack, Vairae forces them to make a DC 22 Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is teleported up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space Vairae can see, and if Vairae is no longer a valid target, the attack misses.

MythicActions.Name: Cages of Wasting
MythicActions.Content: A cage of black energy springs forth around each enemy within 60 feet. Each target must make a DC 22 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 27 (5d10) necrotic damage and is restrained for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn).

MythicActions.Name: My Magic Alone
MythicActions.Content: Vairae unleashes a wave of canceling magic. Each creature within 60 feet must make a DC 22 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a target can’t benefit from magic items or cast spells of 4th level or higher until the end of Vairae’s next turn.

MythicActions.Name: Necrostorm
MythicActions.Content: Vairae fires black bolts of magic. Each creature within 60 feet must make a DC 22 Dexterity saving throw, taking 78 (12d12) necrotic damage on a failed save. A Humanoid killed by this damage rises as a wraith under Vairae’s control at the start of Vairae’s next turn.

Creatures.42i7vkm2.Name: Lich
DamageResistances: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from mundane attacks

Traits.Name: Rejuvenation
Traits.Content:
If the lich is destroyed but their soulstone is intact, their soul retreats into the stone. While the lich’s soul is in the stone, a creature who can see the stone at the start of their turn must succeed on a DC 20 Charisma saving throw or be charmed by the stone until the end of their next turn. While charmed in this way, the creature must do everything in their power to move toward and touch the stone, avoiding obvious hazards.
If a creature touches the stone while the lich’s soul is inside, the creature must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature drops to 0 hit points and the lich reforms with all their hit points in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the soulstone. On a successful save, the creature loses half their hit points and the lich reforms with half their hit points in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the soulstone. The soulstone has AC 19, 50 hit points, and immunity to all damage except force and thunder.

Traits.Name: Supernatural Resistance
Traits.Content: The lich has advantage on saving throws against powers, spells, and other supernatural effects.

Traits.Name: Turn Immunity
Traits.Content: The lich is immune to effects that turn Undead.

Traits.Name: Utility Spells
Traits.Content:
In addition to any other spells in this stat block, the lich can cast the following spells, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 20):
- At will: *arcane lock A, charm person A, detect magic A, detect thoughts A, disguise self A, identify +, locate object A, mage hand A*
- 3/day each: *arcane eye A, arcanist’s magic aura A, contact other plane +, legend lore +, scrying +*
- 1/day each: *forbiddance +, geas +, hallow +, mirage arcane +, symbol +*

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The lich uses Glare of Undeath and makes four attacks using Biting Acid, Disruptive Thunder, Pain of Ages, or a combination of them.

Actions.Name: Biting Acid (3rd-Level Spell)
Actions.Content: Ranged Spell Attack: +12 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (5d8) acid damage, and the target can’t regain hit points until the start of the lich’s next turn.

Actions.Name: Disruptive Thunder (3rd-Level Spell)
Actions.Content: Melee or Ranged Spell Attack: +12 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (4d10) thunder damage. If the target is Huge or smaller, the lich can move them up to 45 feet horizontally.

Actions.Name: Pain of Ages (3rd-Level Spell)
Actions.Content: Melee or Ranged Spell Attack: +12 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 90 ft., one creature. Hit: 21 (6d6) psychic damage, and the first time the target hits a creature with an attack before the start of the lich’s next turn, that attack deals half damage.

Actions.Name: Glare of Undeath
Actions.Content: The lich’s eyes flare with supernatural power at one creature they can see within 60 feet of them. The target must succeed on a DC 20 Wisdom saving throw or be restrained for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn).

Actions.Name: Horrid Woe (2/Day)
Actions.Content: The lich sends negative energy coursing through a creature they can see within 90 feet of them. The target must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw, taking 61 (7d8 + 30) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Actions.Name: Life Absorption (1/Day; 9th-Level Spell; Concentration)
Actions.Content: The lich unleashes a wave of life-sapping energy. Each enemy within 120 feet of them must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 16 (3d10) necrotic damage at the start of each of their turns for 1 minute.

BonusActions.Name: Necrotic Form (3rd-Level Spell)
BonusActions.Content: The lich enters a shadow form and moves up to their speed. While in this form, the lich is immune to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage and the grappled and restrained conditions, and they can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

Reactions.Name: Immortal Insight
Reactions.Content: When another creature the lich can see makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, the lich can cause the roll to be made with advantage or disadvantage.

Traits.Name: Exploit Opening (3/Day)
Traits.Content: When Radlee makes an attack, she has advantage on the attack roll.

Traits.Name: Sure-Footed
Traits.Content: Radlee has advantage on saving throws against being moved against her will or knocked prone.

Traits.Name: Tactical Vigilance
Traits.Content: Radlee has advantage on initiative checks. While she isn’t incapacitated, Radlee and each ally within 30 feet of her who can see or hear her can’t be surprised.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: Radlee makes three Halberd or three Longbow attacks. Alternatively, she makes one Halberd attack and uses Backswipe.

Actions.Name: Halberd
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (1d10 + 7) slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Longbow
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage.

Actions.Name: Backswipe
Actions.Content: Radlee swings her halberd’s shaft at a creature within 5 feet of her. The target must succeed on a DC 18 Strength saving throw or take 16 (3d10) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone.

Reactions.Name: Protector
Reactions.Content: When a creature Radlee can see within 10 feet of her attacks one of her allies, Radlee can make a Halberd attack against the attacker.

Traits.Name: Stone Sacrifice (3/Day)
Traits.Content: When Emer fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead by ending the effects of her Stone Gaze on a creature of her choice within 300 feet of her.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: Emer uses Pinning Shot or makes three attacks using Snake Bite, Longbow, or both. She also uses Stone Gaze or Envenomed Stone, if available.

Actions.Name: Snake Bite
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. Hit: 17 (5d6) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 17 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the end of Emer’s next turn. While poisoned in this way, the target can’t take reactions.

Actions.Name: Longbow
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (1d8 + 7) piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) poison damage.

Actions.Name: Pinning Shot
Actions.Content: Emer makes three Longbow attacks against a creature touching the ground within 150 feet of her. If at least two of those attacks hit, the target is restrained by arrows pinning their limbs to the ground. A creature restrained in this way or another creature who can reach them can use an action to pull out the arrows and end the condition.

Actions.Name: Stone Gaze
Actions.Content:
Emer fires beams of energy from her eyes at up to three creatures she can see within 60 feet of her. Each target must succeed on a DC 17 Constitution saving throw or begin turning to stone (save ends at end of turn). While turning to stone, a creature’s speed is reduced by 10 feet and they have disadvantage on Dexterity checks and saving throws.

If a creature who is turning to stone is targeted by this action again and fails their save, their speed is reduced by another 10 feet and they are dazed until the effect ends.

If a creature who is turning to stone and dazed in this way is targeted by this action again and fails their save, they are petrified and can no longer make saving throws to end the effects of Stone Gaze. The petrification lasts until the creature is restored by Emer’s Stone Sacrifice trait, a cure ailment power of 4th order or higher, a greater restoration spell, or a similar supernatural effect.

Actions.Name: Envenomed Stone (Recharge 5–6)
Actions.Content: Emer utters an ancient hex. Each creature within 60 feet of her who is being turned to stone by her Stone Gaze must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw, taking 44 (8d10) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

BonusActions.Name: Mesmerizing Eyes
BonusActions.Content: The glowing eyes of Emer’s snakes gaze at one creature Emer can see within 60 feet of her. If the target can see Emer, they must succeed on a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw or be charmed until the start of the target’s next turn. A target charmed in this way must immediately use their reaction, if available, to move up to their speed in a direction of Emer’s choice.

Reactions.Name: Blinding Mucus
Reactions.Content: When a creature Emer can see within 5 feet of her hits her with a melee attack, Emer spits venom at their eyes. The target must succeed on a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw or take 7 (2d6) acid damage and be blinded until the start of their next turn.

MythicActions.Name: I See You!
MythicActions.Content: Emer uses Stone Gaze against each enemy she can see within 60 feet of her.

MythicActions.Name: Medusa’s Evolution
MythicActions.Content: Emer grows wings from her back, gaining a flying speed of 40 feet, then she moves up to her speed without provoking opportunity attacks. After 1 minute, the wings crumble to dust and her flying speed is lost.

MythicActions.Name: Stone Puppets
MythicActions.Content: Emer mentally manipulates the stone in each creature who is being turned to stone by her Stone Gaze. Emer and each of those creatures move up to their speed then make a weapon attack against a creature of Emer’s choice (no action required).
