- **Protective Light.** Four brilliant motes orbit the Elemental for 1 minute. When the Elemental deals damage to a creature with an attack, the Elemental can extinguish one mote (no action required) to deal an extra 14 (4d6) radiant damage to that creature.

Traits.Name: Antimagic Susceptibility
Traits.Content: The armor is incapacitated while in the area of an antimagic field. If targeted by dispel magic, the armor must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against the caster's spell save DC or fall unconscious for 1 minute.

Traits.Name: False Appearance
Traits.Content: While the armor remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a normal suit of armor.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The armor makes two melee attacks.

Actions.Name: Slam
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) bludgeoning damage.

Creatures.p14id25q.Name: Decrepit Skeleton
Languages: understands the languages they knew in life but can’t speak

Traits.Name: Bone Weapons
Traits.Content: The skeleton’s weapon attacks are magical. They use their bones as ammunition for their bone bow, regrowing used bones every dusk.

Traits.Name: Minion
Traits.Content: If the skeleton takes damage from an attack or as the result of a failed saving throw, their hit points are reduced to 0. If the skeleton takes damage from another effect, they die if the damage equals or exceeds their hit point maximum; otherwise they take no damage.

Actions.Name: Bone Bow (Group Attack)
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. Hit: 1 piercing damage. If the target is a creature and three or more skeleton minions joined the attack, the skeletons can choose another creature they can see within 5 feet of the original target. The second target takes 2 piercing damage.

Actions.Name: Bone Knife (Group Attack)
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 1 slashing damage.

Traits.Name: Aerial Agility
Traits.Content: While the terrorsaur is flying, their AC is 16.

Traits.Name: Amphibious
Traits.Content: The terrorsaur can breathe air and water.

Traits.Name: Running Start
Traits.Content: The terrorsaur can use only their flying speed if they first either fall 10 feet without hitting the ground or walk 10 feet in a straight line on that turn.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The terrorsaur makes one Bite attack and one Glaive attack.

Actions.Name: Bite
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage.

Actions.Name: Glaive
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Blowgun (Recharge 6)
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one creature. Hit: 1 piercing damage plus 17 (5d6) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn).

Reactions.Name: Reptilian Escape (1/Day)
Reactions.Content: If the terrorsaur fails a Strength or Dexterity saving throw, they lose their tail and succeed instead. They regrow their tail when they finish a long rest.

Reactions.Name: Skulking Retreat
Reactions.Content: If the terrorsaur takes damage from a melee attack, they can move up to half their speed.

Traits.Name: Infernal Ichor
Traits.Content: When the mage dies, their corpse unleashes a spray of burning orange ichor. Each creature within 5 feet of the mage takes 5 fire damage.

Actions.Name: Infernal Teleport
Actions.Content: Melee Spell Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d10) necrotic damage, and if the target is a creature, they must succeed on a DC 14 Charisma saving throw or be teleported up to 15 feet to an unoccupied space the mage can see.

Actions.Name: Rain Hellfire (2nd-Level Spell)
Actions.Content: The mage rains fire down on a point they can see within 60 feet of them. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius sphere centered on that point must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking 10 (3d6) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The fire spreads around corners, and it ignites flammable objects in that area that aren’t being worn or carried.

Actions.Name: Unhallowed Ground (3rd-Level Spell; Concentration)
Actions.Content: The mage chooses a point on the ground that they can see within 60 feet of them. A 20-foot square of glowing, unholy energy appears on the ground centered on that point and lasts for up to 1 minute. Each enemy in that area is vulnerable to fire damage.

Reactions.Name: Spell Siphon
Reactions.Content: When a creature the mage can see within 60 feet of them casts a spell, the mage reflects some of the spell’s magical energy back at the caster. The caster must succeed on a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw or take 3 (1d6) force damage per level of the triggering spell (minimum 1d6 force damage).

Traits.Name: Amphibious
Traits.Content: The scaletooth can breathe air and water.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The scaletooth makes one Bite attack and one Tail attack.

Actions.Name: Bite
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage.

Actions.Name: Tail
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) bludgeoning damage, and if the target is a Medium or smaller creature, the scaletooth can move them 5 feet in any direction or grapple them (escape DC 12). Until this grapple ends, the scaletooth can’t make a Tail attack against another target.

Actions.Name: Rending Bite
Actions.Content: The scaletooth makes a Bite attack with advantage against one creature they have grappled, dealing an extra 3 (1d6) piercing damage on a hit.

Reactions.Name: Reptilian Escape (1/Day)
Reactions.Content: If the scaletooth fails a Strength or Dexterity saving throw, they lose their tail and succeed instead. They regrow their tail when they finish a long rest.

Traits.Name: Deadly Sharpshooter
Traits.Content: Attacking at long range with a ranged weapon doesn’t impose disadvantage on Llyvessa’s attack rolls.

Traits.Name: Elf Glamour (1/Day)
Traits.Content: When a creature targets Llyvessa with an attack, power, or spell, the creature must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by her until the end of her next turn (no action required).

Traits.Name: Mind Link
Traits.Content: Llyvessa can communicate telepathically with any Amethyst Knife boss regardless of distance, provided they are on the same plane of existence.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: Llyvessa makes two Scimitar or Longbow attacks. Alternatively, she makes one Longbow attack and uses Psychic Shot, if available.

Actions.Name: Scimitar
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d6 + 6) slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Longbow
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) piercing damage.

Actions.Name: Psychic Shot (Recharge 5–6)
Actions.Content: Llyvessa makes a Longbow attack that ignores cover, and which can pass through mundane solid objects up to 5 feet thick that aren’t being worn or carried.

BonusActions.Name: Jaunt (1/Day; 3rd-Order Power)
BonusActions.Content: Llyvessa or one willing creature within 5 feet of her teleports to an unoccupied space Llyvessa can see within 30 feet of her.

Reactions.Name: Control Projectiles (3/Day; 2nd-Order Power)
Reactions.Content: When a creature Llyvessa can see within 60 feet of her makes a ranged weapon attack, she gives the creature advantage or disadvantage on the attack roll (her choice).

Reactions.Name: Shared Condition
Reactions.Content: When Llyvessa is blinded, charmed, dazed, deafened, frightened, paralyzed, or stunned, she chooses a willing Amethyst Knife boss within 60 feet of her who doesn’t already have the condition or is immune to it. The chosen creature then gains the condition for the duration, and Llyvessa loses the condition. She can use this reaction even while incapacitated.

Traits.Name: Minion
Traits.Content: If the forcecaller takes damage from an attack or as the result of a failed saving throw, their hit points are reduced to 0. If the forcecaller takes damage from another effect, they die if the damage equals or exceeds their hit point maximum; otherwise they take no damage.

Traits.Name: Relentless Minion
Traits.Content: When the forcecaller is reduced to 0 hit points while they aren’t incapacitated, they can deal 1 force damage to an enemy within 5 feet of them.

Actions.Name: Chainspoken (Group Attack)
Actions.Content: Melee or Ranged Spell Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30/60 ft., one target. Hit: 1 force damage. If two or more forcecallers joined the attack, they can deal the same amount of force damage to another creature within 10 feet of the target.

Creatures.q2q8uzmk.Name: Marilith Decimator
DamageResistances: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks

Traits.Name: Reactive
Traits.Content: The marilith regains its reaction at the beginning of each turn.

Traits.Name: More Fearsome Than Death
Traits.Content: If another demon within 60 feet would fail a Wisdom saving throw, the marilith can choose for it to succeed. If it does, the demon is frightened of the marilith until the end of its next turn.

Traits.Name: Magic Resistance
Traits.Content: The marilith has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The marilith makes seven melee attacks, one of which can be with its Constrict.

Actions.Name: Claws
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) slashing damage. Instead of dealing damage, the marilith can grapple the target (escape DC 17).

Actions.Name: Trophytaker Blades
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) slashing damage. A creature killed by this attack is beheaded.

Actions.Name: Constrict
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) bludgeoning damage, and the target is grappled (escape DC 17). A creature grappled in this way is restrained.

Reactions.Name: Deflect
Reactions.Content: When it is hit with a weapon attack, the marilith can add +5 to its AC against the triggering attack.

Reactions.Name: Overwhelm
Reactions.Content: When it misses a creature it is grappling with a melee attack, the marilith can add +5 to its attack roll.

Reactions.Name: Tail Lash
Reactions.Content: When a creature enters its reach, the marilith can force the creature to succeed on a DC 17 Strength saving throw or be pushed 10 feet away and knocked prone.

Reactions.Name: Crush
Reactions.Content: When a creature the marilith has grappled with its Constrict speaks or casts a spell with a verbal component, the marilith can crush the air out of its lungs, causing it to immediately begin suffocating.

Reactions.Name: Flickering Charge
Reactions.Content: When the marilith succeeds on a saving throw against a spell, the marilith can magically teleport up to 30 feet directly toward the spell's caster.

Traits.Name: Crafty
Traits.Content: The warrior doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks when they move out of an enemy’s reach.

Actions.Name: Shortsword
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage.

Actions.Name: Shortbow
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage.

Reactions.Name: Fleet Foot
Reactions.Content: When a creature within 5 feet of the warrior misses them with a melee attack, the warrior can move up to half their speed.

Traits.Name: Crush Underfoot
Traits.Content: When the wind sprinter enters a Medium or smaller enemy’s space, the enemy must choose to either fall prone or take 10 (3d6) bludgeoning damage. Each enemy can be affected by this trait only once per turn.

Traits.Name: Oncoming Storm
Traits.Content: Wisps of stinging snow and icy winds surround the wind sprinter. When an enemy concentrating on a power, a spell, or a similar effect starts their turn within 15 feet of the wind sprinter, the enemy must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or lose concentration.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The wind sprinter makes two Ice Axe attacks.

Actions.Name: Ice Axe
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (3d10 + 6) slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Rock
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, range 60/240 ft., one target. Hit: 28 (4d10 + 6) bludgeoning damage.

Actions.Name: Blizzard Surge (Recharge 5–6)
Actions.Content: The wind sprinter moves up to their speed in a straight line. At any point during this move, they can make up to three Ice Axe attacks against different creatures.

Reactions.Name: Begone, Smallfolk!
Reactions.Content: After a Medium or smaller creature makes an opportunity attack against the wind sprinter, the wind sprinter can make an Ice Axe attack against that creature.

Traits.Name: Acid Burst
Traits.Content: When the tadpole is reduced to 0 hit points, their body pops, spraying acid around them. Each creature within 5 feet of the tadpole takes 1 acid damage.

Traits.Name: Amphibious
Traits.Content: The tadpole can breathe air and water.

Traits.Name: Minion
Traits.Content: If the tadpole takes damage from an attack or as the result of a failed saving throw, their hit points are reduced to 0. If the tadpole takes damage from another effect, they die if the damage equals or exceeds their hit point maximum; otherwise they take no damage.

Traits.Name: Noxious Skin
Traits.Content: When a creature starts their turn within 5 feet of three or more tadpoles, that creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the start of their next turn. The DC for this saving throw equals 10 plus the number of tadpoles within 5 feet of the creature.

Actions.Name: Bite (Group Attack)
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, one target. Hit: 1 piercing damage.

Traits.Name: Minion
Traits.Content: If the whelp takes damage from an attack or as the result of a failed saving throw, their hit points are reduced to 0. If the whelp takes damage from another effect, they die if the damage equals or exceeds their hit point maximum; otherwise they take no damage.

Traits.Name: Relentless Hunger
Traits.Content: When the whelp is reduced to 0 hit points by any damage other than acid or fire damage, they don’t die or fall unconscious, and can continue moving and taking actions as usual. The whelp only dies if they end their turn with 0 hit points, if acid or fire damage reduces them to 0 hit points, or if they take acid or fire damage while they have 0 hit points.

Traits.Name: Ripping Claws
Traits.Content: Spaces within reach of one or more whelps are difficult terrain for Medium or smaller enemies. When an enemy leaves the reach of one or more whelps who can see them, the enemy takes 2 slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Bite (Group Attack)
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 piercing damage, and if the target is not a Construct, an Elemental, or a Plant, each whelp who joined this attack regains all lost hit points.

Creatures.qd4x78et.Name: Garlo "Brickjaw" Mudclap

Traits.Name: Relentless Form
Traits.Content: If Garlo takes 30 damage or less that would reduce him to 0 hit points, he is reduced to 1 hit point instead. Once used, this trait recharges after a short or long rest.

Traits.Name: Unshakable Code
Traits.Content: Garlo cannot be compelled to act against his nature by charm, fear, suggestion, or magical persuasion.

Traits.Name: Row’s Vow
Traits.Content: While in The Row, Garlo has advantage on all saving throws and cannot be moved or knocked prone against his will.

Actions.Name: Inevitable Fist
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: automatic hit. Hit: 25 bludgeoning damage. This blow cannot be reduced or redirected by reactions or features that rely on being hit or missed. The first time Garlo lands this hit on a creature, they must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or be Staggered (disadvantage on their next attack roll and movement halved until the end of their next turn).

Actions.Name: The Hullbreaker (1/Short Rest)
Actions.Content: Garlo makes an Inevitable Fist attack that deals 50 bludgeoning damage instead of 25. The target is automatically Staggered. If reduced to 0 HP by this attack, the target is launched 10 feet and falls prone.

Actions.Name: Stare Down (Recharge 5–6)
Actions.Content: Garlo fixes his eyes on a creature within 30 feet. That creature must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be frightened until the end of their next turn. On a success, the creature is immune to this effect for 24 hours.

Creatures.qd4x78et.Description: The reigning champion of The Row. A silent, immovable force with fists like anchors and a reputation that leaves challengers shaking.

Traits.Name: Shapechanger
Traits.Content: The doppelganger can use its action to polymorph into a Small or Medium humanoid it has seen, or back into its true form. Its statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

Traits.Name: Ambusher
Traits.Content: In the first round of a combat, the doppelganger has advantage on attack rolls against any creature it surprised

Traits.Name: Surprise Attack
Traits.Content: If the doppelganger surprises a creature and hits it with an attack during the first round of combat, the target takes an extra 10 (3d6) damage from the attack.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The doppelganger makes two melee attacks.

Actions.Name: Slam
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) bludgeoning damage.

Actions.Name: Read Thoughts
Actions.Content: The doppelganger magically reads the surface thoughts of one creature within 60 ft. of it. The effect can penetrate barriers, but 3 ft. of wood or dirt, 2 ft. of stone, 2 inches of metal, or a thin sheet of lead blocks it. While the target is in range, the doppelganger can continue reading its thoughts, as long as the doppelganger's concentration isn't broken (as if concentrating on a spell). While reading the target's mind, the doppelganger has advantage on Wisdom (Insight) and Charisma (Deception, Intimidation, and Persuasion) checks against the target.

Traits.Name: Exploit Opening (3/Day)
Traits.Content: When the knave makes an attack, they have advantage on the attack roll.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The knave makes two Morningstar or two Javelin attacks. They can replace one attack with a Shield Bash attack.

Actions.Name: Morningstar
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) piercing damage, or 13 (2d8 + 4) piercing damage if the target is prone.

Actions.Name: Shield Bash
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d4 + 4) bludgeoning damage, and if the target is a Medium or smaller creature, they must succeed on a DC 14 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

Actions.Name: Javelin
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage.

BonusActions.Name: Stay Down
BonusActions.Content: The knave kicks one prone creature within 5 feet of them. The target must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or their speed is reduced to 0 until the end of their next turn.

Traits.Name: Bugbear’s Inspiration
Traits.Content: Allied Humanoids within 15 feet of the roughneck who can see or hear them have advantage on Wisdom and Charisma saving throws.

Actions.Name: Multiattack (Battle Fury Only)
Actions.Content: The roughneck makes two Handaxe attacks.

Actions.Name: Handaxe
Actions.Content: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Bugbear Battle Cry (1/Day)
Actions.Content: Each ally within 15 feet of the roughneck who can hear and understand them gains advantage on attack rolls until the start of the roughneck’s next turn.

BonusActions.Name: Battle Fury (1/Day)
BonusActions.Content: The roughneck enters a furious state that lasts for 1 minute. While in this state, the roughneck can take the Multiattack action on their turn, and they have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from mundane attacks.

Traits.Name: Heat and Pressure
Traits.Content: When an enemy moves out of the red fist’s reach, the enemy must succeed on a DC 18 Constitution saving throw or gain a level of exhaustion. Creatures with resistance or immunity to fire damage are immune to this effect.

Traits.Name: Molten Flesh
Traits.Content: The first time a creature other than a fire giant touches the red fist or hits them with a melee attack on a turn, that creature takes 7 (2d6) fire damage.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The red fist makes two Unarmed Strike attacks.

Actions.Name: Unarmed Strike
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 21 (4d6 + 7) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) fire damage. If the same creature is hit twice with this attack on a turn, they are blinded until the end of their next turn.

Actions.Name: Hurl Flame
Actions.Content: Ranged Spell Attack: +10 to hit, range 180 ft., one target. Hit: 34 (8d6 + 6) fire damage.

Reactions.Name: Guardian Block (3/Day)
Reactions.Content: When an enemy within 10 feet of the red fist hits a creature other than the red fist with an attack, the red fist halves the damage dealt by that attack. The red fist then makes an Unarmed Strike against the attacker.

Creatures.qm2zr7jc.Name: Sandstrider (Dawnrider)

Traits.Name: Sunflash Technique (3/day)
Traits.Content: As a Bonus Action, the Dawnrider flashes sunlight from mirrored blades. All creatures within 15 ft. that can see must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution save or be blinded until the start of the Dawnrider’s next turn.

Traits.Name: Lightbender armor (3/day)
Traits.Content: As a Reaction when targeted by an attack, the Dawnrider gains +2 AC until the start of its next turn and can move 10 ft. without provoking opportunity attacks as the light plays tricks on the attacker.

Traits.Name: Solar Conviction (1/day)
Traits.Content: As an Action, the Dawnrider channels solar heat through its weapon. On a hit, the attack deals an extra 2d8 fire damage and the target must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution save or catch fire, taking 1d6 fire damage at the start of each turn for 1 minute (save ends).

Actions.Name: Scimitar
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (3d6+3) slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Shortbow
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6+3) piercing damage.

Creatures.qm2zr7jc.Description: Elite desert skirmishers who dance with the dunes and wield the sun’s glare as their shield.

Traits.Name: Multiple Heads
Traits.Content: The marsh hydra has advantage on saving throws against being blinded, charmed, dazed, deafened, frightened, stunned, and knocked unconscious.

Traits.Name: Roll Over
Traits.Content: If the marsh hydra moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits them with an Entangling Serpents attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 16 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The marsh hydra makes two Entangling Serpents attacks. They can replace one attack with a Many Bites attack.

Actions.Name: Entangling Serpents
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 5) bludgeoning damage, and the target is grappled (escape DC 16).

Actions.Name: Many Bites
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d8 + 5) piercing damage, and the target must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 18 (4d8) poison damage and is poisoned until the end of the marsh hydra’s next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage and isn’t poisoned.

BonusActions.Name: Serpent Surprise
BonusActions.Content:
The marsh hydra activates one of the following effects; if the effect targets another creature, the marsh hydra targets one creature they can see within 30 feet of them:
1. Poison Spit. The targeted creature must succeed on a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw or take 9 (2d8) poison damage and be blinded until the end of the marsh hydra’s next turn.
2. Entrancing Gaze. The targeted creature must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by the marsh hydra for 1 minute or until the marsh hydra harms the target (save ends at end of turn).
3. Terrifying Rattle. The targeted creature must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or be frightened by the marsh hydra for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn).
4. Reinforcements. The marsh hydra shuffles which heads are on the outside of their body and regains 10 (3d6) hit points.

Creatures.qrtrt1p7.Name: Crab Folk

Traits.Name: Limited Amphibiousness
Traits.Content: The crab folk can breathe air and water, but it needs to be submerged once every 24 hours to avoid suffocating.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The crab folk makes two claw attacks.

Actions.Name: Claw
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) bludgeoning damage and the target is grappled (escape DC 14). Until this grapple ends, the crab folk can automatically hit the target with its claw, and the crab folk can’t make attacks with that claw against other targets. The crab folk has two claws, each of which can grapple only one target.

Creatures.qrtrt1p7.Description:
The crab folk are the result of a magical ritual used long ago by a mighty green hag to transform her ogre minions into more useful servants. All crab folk that now exist are descended from those original creations, with most living in small, isolated clutches. Though they are content to lead peaceful lives as hunters and gatherers, the magic that created the crab folk sometimes compels them to lash out in fearsome raids when the conditions are right.

**Aquatic Vassals**. Long ago, a green hag gathered a small army of ogres to serve as her enforcers along the desolate coast she had claimed. The hag found the ogres’ simple minds and brutal strength useful, but too often, their lack of cunning foiled her plans. Many tumbled into the sea and drowned, too simpleminded and impatient to learn how to swim. Others smashed caravans and crushed their defenders most effectively, but looted cheap, bulky trade goods while leaving valuable smaller treasures behind. Frustrated by her servants, the hag wove a spell to adapt them to the coastal terrain while planting in their minds a few simple commands to help them with their tasks. She gave them the tenacity and toughness of a crab to augment their strength, grasping claws to help them claim the treasures she loved, and a deeply embedded magical compulsion to obedience.

**Treasure Hungry**. The hag had a particular love of silver, and was loath to allow even the smallest bit of that metal to slip through her grasp. Her spell gave the crab folk an insatiable love for silver. When they see it, all crab folk feel an irresistible urge to seize it and carry it back to their lairs. If necessary, they resort to violence to claim the metal.

**Brutal Raiders**. Each year, when the warm summer gives way to the chill of autumn, the crab folk look to the sky for omens. In ancient days, the green hag kept close watch on the fishing villages and towns near her domain. If those settlements grew too strong, she sent her familiar, a cruel seagull, to harangue the crab folk with its cries and drive them to war. Today, by a stroke of bad luck a gull might happen to land near a crab folk lair and utter the right combination of cries to summon up this ancient compulsion. Driven to action, the crab folk rampage along the coast, looting and smashing any settlements they know of or encounter. After six days exactly, their rampage ends, and they return to their humble lives.

**Deep-Seated Memories**. When a coastal raid or a theft of silver is done, crab folk gather the treasures they have won and place them at the feet of a simple effigy of their hag creator, made from driftwood and other scrap and kept in their communal lair. The crab folk have little sense of what drives them, but they understand that the effigy represents a mother and creator who must be obeyed. Legend holds that the green hag preferred to travel in the guise of a regal hobgoblin queen. If the crab folk ever encounter someone who bears a strong resemblance to that form, their magical compulsion might force them to worship that creature, obeying their every command.

Traits.Name: Bloodstones (3 Uses)
Traits.Content: When Baron Uthrak fails a saving throw while holding his magic longsword Nine Lives, he can choose to succeed instead. If he does so, he takes 7 (2d6) necrotic damage as one of the bloodstones set in the sword’s pommel fills with his blood.

Traits.Name: Exploit Opening (3/Day)
Traits.Content: When Baron Uthrak makes an attack, he has advantage on the attack roll.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: Baron Uthrak makes two attacks using Nine Lives, Whip, or both.

Actions.Name: Nine Lives
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d8 + 5) slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) necrotic damage.

Actions.Name: Whip
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d4 + 4) slashing damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 15 feet toward Baron Uthrak.

BonusActions.Name: Kneel, Peasant!
BonusActions.Content: Baron Uthrak kicks a Large or smaller creature within 5 feet of him. The target must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or take 6 (1d4 + 4) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone.

Reactions.Name: Riposte
Reactions.Content: When a creature Baron Uthrak can see misses him with a melee attack, Baron Uthrak makes a melee attack against that creature.

MythicActions.Name: Shoot!
MythicActions.Content: Each ally within 60 feet of Baron Uthrak who can hear him can make a ranged weapon attack (no action required).

MythicActions.Name: Form Up!
MythicActions.Content: Baron Uthrak and each ally within 60 feet of him who can hear him can move up to their speed without provoking opportunity attacks. Baron Uthrak and each ally enters a defensive stance that lasts until that creature moves or is restrained or incapacitated. When two or more allies in a defensive stance are within 5 feet of each other, attack rolls against them are made with disadvantage.

MythicActions.Name: Lead from the Front
MythicActions.Content: Baron Uthrak moves up to twice his speed without provoking opportunity attacks. During or after this movement, he can make up to four Nine Lives or Whip attacks with advantage, each against a different target. After he attacks a target in this way, each ally within 5 feet of that target can make a melee attack with advantage against the same target (no action required).

Creatures.qxyayh2r.Name: Servok War Engine
DamageResistances: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from mundane attacks

Traits.Name: Immutable Form
Traits.Content: The engine is immune to any power, spell, or effect that would alter their form.

Traits.Name: Siege Monster
Traits.Content: The engine deals double damage to objects and structures.

Traits.Name: Supernatural Resistance
Traits.Content: The engine has advantage on saving throws against powers, spells, and other supernatural effects.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The engine makes two attacks using Blade Rake, Force Cannon, or both.

Actions.Name: Blade Rake
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +14 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 35 (4d12 + 9) slashing damage. If the target is Large or smaller, they are pulled up to 15 feet toward the engine.

Actions.Name: Force Cannon
Actions.Content: Ranged Spell Attack: +12 to hit, range 400 ft., one target. Hit: 33 (6d10) force damage, and the target is dazed until the end of their next turn.

Actions.Name: Burning Oil
Actions.Content: The engine shoots burning oil in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 52 (15d6) fire damage and is covered in scalding oil. A creature covered in scalding oil takes 10 (3d6) fire damage at the start of each of their turns. A creature can use an action to remove the oil from themself or a creature they can reach, ending the effect. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and is not covered in scalding oil.

BonusActions.Name: Destructive Rollout
BonusActions.Content: The engine moves up to their speed in a straight line, destroying mundane walls in their path that are less than 10 feet thick. Medium or smaller mundane objects in the engine’s path are destroyed if the objects aren’t being worn or carried by a creature. The engine can move through the spaces of other creatures during this movement as if they were difficult terrain, and if the engine does so, the creature must succeed on a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw or take 11 (2d10) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone.

Traits.Name: Hold Breath
Traits.Content: The clawfish can hold their breath for 15 minutes.

Actions.Name: Claw
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) slashing damage, and if the target is a Small or larger creature, the clawfish can pull themself into the target’s space and attach to the target. While attached, the clawfish moves with the target without provoking opportunity attacks, and the clawfish can’t make Claw attacks. A creature who can reach the clawfish can use an action to make a DC 12 Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check. On a success, the clawfish is detached and shunted into an unoccupied space of the clawfish’s choice within 5 feet of the target. The clawfish also detaches if they become grappled, incapacitated, prone, or restrained, or if they use 5 feet of movement to detach.

Actions.Name: Electrocute (Recharge 6)
Actions.Content: Each creature touching the clawfish takes 7 (2d6) lightning damage. If the clawfish is immersed in water, each other creature within 10 feet of the clawfish who is touching the same body of water must succeed on a DC 11 Dexterity saving throw or take the same damage.

Traits.Name: Nimble Escape
Traits.Content: The goblin can take the Disengage or Hide action as a bonus action on each of its turns.

Actions.Name: Scimitar
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Throw stew
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) poison damage.

Actions.Name: Yegg can throw stew as a bonus action

Traits.Name: Relentless (1/Turn)
Traits.Content: When the bloodrunner isn’t incapacitated and they are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, they can make an attack against an enemy (no action required) before the hit point reduction is resolved. If the attack hits and its damage reduces the target to 0 hit points, the bloodrunner drops to 1 hit point instead of 0 hit points.

Traits.Name: Unimpeded
Traits.Content: The bloodrunner can occupy a prone creature’s space. The first time the bloodrunner enters a prone creature’s space on the bloodrunner’s turn, the prone creature takes 3 (1d6) bludgeoning damage. When a prone creature in the bloodrunner’s space stands up, the creature enters the nearest unoccupied space of their choice.

Actions.Name: Spiked Shield
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) piercing damage. If the bloodrunner moves at least 10 feet before hitting a Large or smaller creature with this attack, the bloodrunner can choose to knock the target prone or push them up to 10 feet away.

Actions.Name: Javelin
Actions.Content: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage.

Reactions.Name: Juke
Reactions.Content: When the bloodrunner makes a Dexterity saving throw, they can gain advantage on the save. To do so, the bloodrunner can’t be prone, grappled, or restrained.

Traits.Name: Avoidance
Traits.Content: If the lightbender is subjected to an effect that allows them to make a saving throw to take only half damage, they instead take no damage if they succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if they fail.

Traits.Name: Pounce
Traits.Content: If the lightbender moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits them with a Claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the lightbender can make one Bite attack against them as a bonus action.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The lightbender makes one Bite attack and two Claw attacks, or uses their Tail Whip twice.

Actions.Name: Bite
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) piercing damage.

Actions.Name: Claw
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Tail Whip
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) radiant damage.

Actions.Name: Hypnotic Mane (1/Day)
Actions.Content: The lightbender discharges the light absorbed in their mane into a brilliant, mesmerizing display. Each creature within 15 feet of the lightbender must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by the lightbender for 1 minute. While charmed in this way, a creature is incapacitated and has a speed of 0. If a creature charmed in this way takes damage or if someone else uses an action to shake the creature out of their stupor, the condition ends for that creature.

Reactions.Name: Afterimage
Reactions.Content: When the lightbender is hit by an attack, they can reveal that the attacker is attacking a past visual imprint of the lightbender. The lightbender appears in an unoccupied space they can see within 30 feet of their imprint, the attack misses, then the imprint disappears. The lightbender can’t use this reaction if the attacker relies on senses other than sight, such as blindsight, or if they can perceive illusions as false, as with truesight.

Actions.Name: Claw
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Earth Bump
Actions.Content: As the mohler burrows under a Large or smaller creature on the ground within 5 feet of them, the mohler shifts the ground. The creature above them must succeed on a DC 12 Dexterity saving throw or be knocked prone.

Actions.Name: Ground Grinder
Actions.Content: The mohler burrows up to their burrowing speed. Ground within 5 feet of where the mohler burrows becomes difficult terrain.

Reactions.Name: Zip
Reactions.Content: If the mohler fails a Dexterity saving throw while burrowing, they succeed instead.

Creatures.rgi3k0gm.Name: Shtriga Nonna
DamageResistances: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from mundane attacks

Traits.Name: Feline Resilience (3/Day)
Traits.Content: When Shtriga Nonna fails a saving throw, she can immediately turn into a cat of her size (Medium in her normal form, Large while under the effect of Grow) and succeed instead. While in cat form, Shtriga Nonna retains her game statistics and ability to speak, except she can’t cast spells or use Soul Steal. She reverts to her previous form at the end of her next turn.

Traits.Name: Supernatural Resistance
Traits.Content: Shtriga Nonna has advantage on saving throws against powers, spells, and other supernatural effects.

Traits.Name: Utility Spells
Traits.Content:
In addition to any other spells in this stat block, Shtriga Nonna can cast the following spells, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15):
At will: alter self, detect thoughts, thaumaturgy
3/day each: fabricate, legend lore
1/day: scrying

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: Shtriga Nonna makes two Corrosive Claw attacks. She can replace one attack with a use of Soul Steal, if available.

Actions.Name: Corrosive Claw
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft. (10 ft. with Grow), one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) slashing damage, or 14 (3d6 + 4) slashing damage while under the effect of Grow. If the target is wearing mundane metal armor, they must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity throw or their armor takes a permanent and cumulative −1 penalty to the AC it offers. If the armor is reduced to an AC of 10, it is destroyed.

Actions.Name: Soul Steal (Recharge 5–6)
Actions.Content: Shtriga Nonna inhales deeply in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw, taking 16 (3d10) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Shtriga Nonna regains 10 hit points for each creature who fails this saving throw.

BonusActions.Name: Grow (2/Day; 3rd-Level Spell)
BonusActions.Content: For 1 minute, Shtriga Nonna magically increases in size, along with anything she is wearing or carrying. While enlarged, she is Large, her reach becomes 10 feet, she deals an extra 3 (1d6) damage on Strength-based weapon attacks (included in the attacks), and she makes Strength checks and Strength saving throws with advantage. If Shtriga Nonna lacks the room to become Large, she attains the maximum size possible in the space available.

BonusActions.Name: Gobble You Up
BonusActions.Content: Shtriga Nonna feeds on the body of an unconscious creature within 5 feet of her. The target takes 10 (3d6) piercing damage, and Shtriga Nonna recharges her Soul Steal action.

Reactions.Name: Turned Upside Down
Reactions.Content: When a creature hits Shtriga Nonna with a melee attack, she magically reverses gravity around her. Each creature within 5 feet of her must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or rise vertically 10 feet into the air. A creature levitating in this way remains suspended until the end of their next turn, then falls unless they have an ability that keeps them aloft.

MythicActions.Name: Snackies for Sweeties
MythicActions.Content: The air within 30 feet of Shtriga Nonna magically fills with the scent of rotten food. Each other creature in that area must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be poisoned for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn). Creatures are affected even if they hold their breath or don’t need to breathe.

MythicActions.Name: Feline Felicity
MythicActions.Content: Shtriga Nonna grows four cat legs in a burst of magical energy. Each creature within 5 feet of her must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 10 (4d4) force damage and is pushed 10 feet away from her. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn’t pushed. Shtriga Nonna can then move up to twice her speed, after which the extra cat legs disappear.

MythicActions.Name: Open the Oven
MythicActions.Content: Shtriga Nonna opens a portal to her massive oven in the palms of her hands, creating a blast of heat and noise in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking 13 (3d8) fire damage plus 13 (3d8) thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Creatures.rgrpzfi6.Name: Blood-Starved Vampire
Languages: the languages they knew in life

Traits.Name: Minion
Traits.Content: If the vampire takes damage from an attack or as the result of a failed saving throw, their hit points are reduced to 0. If the vampire takes damage from another effect, they die if the damage equals or exceeds their hit point maximum; otherwise they take no damage.

Traits.Name: Radiant Weakening
Traits.Content: The vampire has disadvantage on saving throws against effects that deal radiant damage.

Actions.Name: Feeding Frenzy (Group Attack)
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 2 piercing damage plus 3 necrotic damage. If two or more vampires joined the attack, the target’s hit point maximum is reduced by a number equal to the necrotic damage dealt. The reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest or is targeted by a cure ailment power, a lesser restoration spell, or a similar supernatural effect. The target dies if this attack reduces their hit point maximum to 0.

Traits.Name: Exceptional Smell
Traits.Content: The bear has a +10 bonus to Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell, and they can smell creatures even through dirt, snow, and water.

Traits.Name: Natural Camouflage
Traits.Content: The bear has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made in their native terrain.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The bear makes one Bite attack and one Claw attack.

Actions.Name: Bite
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) piercing damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, they are grappled (escape DC 14). Until this grapple ends, the bear can’t use their Bite attack on another target.

Actions.Name: Claw
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d4 + 4) slashing damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, they are knocked prone.

Actions.Name: Powerful Swipe (Recharge 6)
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (1d12 + 4) bludgeoning damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, they are shoved up to 10 feet away and knocked prone. If the target hits a solid surface, they take an extra 7 (2d6) bludgeoning damage.

Creatures.ri6h4uvk.Name: Marble Stone Giant
DamageResistances: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from mundane attacks that aren’t adamantine

Traits.Name: Agility Rune
Traits.Content: Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn’t impose disadvantage on the giant’s ranged attack rolls.

Traits.Name: False Appearance
Traits.Content: While motionless, the giant is indistinguishable from a statue.

Traits.Name: Stone Flesh
Traits.Content: When a creature attacks the giant with a melee weapon that isn’t adamantine and rolls a 1 on the d20 for that attack, that weapon is destroyed if it is mundane. If that weapon is supernatural, it loses all supernatural properties for 1 hour.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The giant makes two Greatsling attacks.

Actions.Name: Greatsling
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, range 60/240 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (3d6 + 5) bludgeoning damage.

Actions.Name: Slam
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 27 (4d10 + 5) bludgeoning damage, and if the target is a creature, they must succeed on a DC 16 Strength saving throw or be pushed 10 feet away from the giant.

Actions.Name: Boulder Roll (Recharge 5–6)
Actions.Content: The giant bowls a boulder in a 10-foot-wide, 30-foot-long line along the ground. Each creature in this line must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 27 (5d10) bludgeoning damage and falls prone. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage and doesn’t fall prone. Each object in this line that isn’t supernatural or worn or carried by a creature takes 27 (5d10) bludgeoning damage. At the end of its roll, the boulder crumbles, creating a 10-foot square of difficult terrain.

BonusActions.Name: Siege Stance
BonusActions.Content: The giant digs their heels into the ground, reducing their speed to 0. While in this stance, the normal and long ranges of the giant’s Greatsling attack are doubled, and when this attack hits, each other creature within 5 feet of the target must succeed on a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw or take 10 (3d6) bludgeoning damage. The giant’s stance lasts until they become grappled, incapacitated, prone, or restrained, or until the giant ends it as a bonus action.

Traits.Name: Battle Tranquility (3/Day)
Traits.Content: When Aastrika fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead. When she does so, she can’t use Sweltering Heat until the end of her next turn.

Traits.Name: Molten Flesh
Traits.Content: The first time a creature other than a fire giant touches Aastrika or hits her with a melee attack on a turn, that creature takes 7 (2d6) fire damage.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: Aastrika takes the Disengage action and makes up to three attacks using Unarmed Strike, Hurl Flame, or both.

Actions.Name: Unarmed Strike
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 21 (4d6 + 7) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) fire damage. If the target is a creature, they are outlined in red light until the start of Aastrika’s next turn, and for the duration, attack rolls against them have advantage.

Actions.Name: Hurl Flame
Actions.Content: Ranged Spell Attack: +10 to hit, range 180 ft., one target. Hit: 20 (4d6 + 6) fire damage.

Actions.Name: Lava Pillar (Recharge 5–6)
Actions.Content: Aastrika causes a 20-foot-radius, 60-foot-high cylinder of lava to erupt from the ground at a point she can see within 120 feet of her. Each creature in that area must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking 38 (11d6) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

BonusActions.Name: Sweltering Heat
BonusActions.Content: Aastrika’s inner flame pulses harshly. Each creature within 20 feet of her who doesn’t have resistance or immunity to fire damage must succeed on a DC 18 Constitution saving throw or gain a level of exhaustion.

Reactions.Name: Power of Flame
Reactions.Content: When another fire giant Aastrika can see within 60 feet of her misses with a weapon attack, Aastrika causes flame to trail from the attacker. The attacker rolls damage for the attack as if they had hit, and the target takes half that amount as fire damage instead of taking the full amount of normal damage.

MythicActions.Name: Forward!
MythicActions.Content: Aastrika and up to three creatures she can see can move up to their speed without provoking opportunity attacks and make one melee attack each.

MythicActions.Name: The Manifold Self
MythicActions.Content: Aastrika disappears in a flash of light and teleports, reappearing in an unoccupied space she can see within 30 feet of her. At the same time, five fire giant troopers appear in unoccupied spaces within 30 feet of the space she teleported from. The troopers act immediately after this mythic action is taken and on the same initiative count in subsequent rounds.

MythicActions.Name: This. Ends. Now.
MythicActions.Content: Aastrika and each willing fire giant within 60 feet of her unleashes a burst of heat. Each creature within 10 feet of Aastrika or one of these fire giants must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking 55 (10d10) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Traits.Name: Infernal Ichor
Traits.Content: When a creature within 5 feet of the recruit reduces the recruit to 0 hit points, the recruit’s corpse unleashes a spray of burning orange ichor. The creature who reduced the recruit to 0 hit points takes 1 fire damage.

Traits.Name: Minion
Traits.Content: If the recruit takes damage from an attack or as the result of a failed saving throw, their hit points are reduced to 0. If the recruit takes damage from another effect, they die if the damage equals or exceeds their hit point maximum; otherwise they take no damage.

Traits.Name: Tactical Positioning
Traits.Content: When a non-minion ally attacks a creature, that ally gains a +1 bonus to the attack roll for each recruit within 5 feet of the target (maximum bonus of +5).

Actions.Name: Longsword (Group Attack)
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 1 slashing damage.

Traits.Name: Dying Gasp
Traits.Content: When the Horror dies, they begin to rupture in a vile explosion of viscera and necrotic energy. The creature who reduced the Horror to 0 hit points can make a DC 15 Charisma (Persuasion) check to appeal to Elyas’s departing soul (no action required). On a successful check, his soul transforms the explosion and each enemy of the Grave Order within 30 feet of the Horror gains 7 (2d6) temporary hit points. On a failed check (or if no check is made), each creature within 15 feet of the Horror must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw, taking 4 (1d8) acid damage plus 4 (1d8) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Actions.Name: Spine Cleaver
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) slashing damage, or 13 (2d8 + 4) slashing damage against a grappled target.

Actions.Name: Bone Hook
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, range 15 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, they are grappled (escape DC 14) and the Horror can pull the target up to 15 feet toward them. Until this grapple ends, the Horror can’t make a Bone Hook attack against another target.

BonusActions.Name: Valor’s Death
BonusActions.Content: Each enemy within 10 feet of the Horror who can hear the Horror must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or be frightened of the Horror for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn). If a creature’s saving throw is successful or the effect ends for them, the creature is immune to the Horror’s Valor’s Death for 24 hours.

Traits.Name: Flyby
Traits.Content: Sair doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks when they fly out of an enemy’s reach.

Actions.Name: Claws
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Psionic Bolt (1st-Order Power)
Actions.Content: Ranged Power Attack: +6 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (3d6) psychic damage. If the target is Large or smaller, they are pushed 5 feet away from Sair.

Actions.Name: Mind Scream (1/Day; 3rd-Order Power)
Actions.Content: Sair psionically pierces the mind of each enemy within 30 feet of them. Each target must make a DC 14 Intelligence saving throw, taking 17 (5d6) psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature reduced to 0 hit points by this damage is automatically stabilized.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The bonesplitter makes two attacks using Bite, Spiked Flail, or both. They can replace one attack with a use of Bloody Roar, if available.

Actions.Name: Bite
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage, or 11 (2d6 + 4) piercing damage if the target is restrained.

Actions.Name: Spiked Flail
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, they are grappled (escape DC 14). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained and the bonesplitter can’t make Spiked Flail attacks.

Actions.Name: Bloody Roar (Recharge 6)
Actions.Content: The bonesplitter roars, spitting a spray of blood. Each ally within 10 feet of them who can see them can make a weapon attack (no action required), provided that ally isn’t incapacitated.

BonusActions.Name: Over Here!
BonusActions.Content: The bonesplitter yanks a creature they are grappling. The target must succeed on a DC 14 Strength saving throw or swap spaces with the bonesplitter.

Reactions.Name: Death Frenzy
Reactions.Content: When an ally the bonesplitter can see within 30 feet of them is reduced to 0 hit points, the bonesplitter moves up to half their speed and makes a Bite attack.

Creatures.s25fxxv6.Name: Servok Builder
DamageResistances: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from mundane attacks

Traits.Name: Immutable Form
Traits.Content: The builder is immune to any power, spell, or effect that would alter their form.

Traits.Name: Siege Monster
Traits.Content: The builder deals double damage to objects and structures.

Traits.Name: Supernatural Resistance
Traits.Content: The builder has advantage on saving throws against powers, spells, and other supernatural effects.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The builder makes two attacks using Wrecking Ball, Stone, or both, and can use Lay the Foundation, if available.

Actions.Name: Wrecking Ball
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 26 (3d12 + 7) bludgeoning damage. Each enemy within 5 feet of the target must succeed on a DC 19 Dexterity saving throw or take 13 (2d12) bludgeoning damage.

Actions.Name: Stone
Actions.Content: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 10 ft. or range 60/240 ft., one target. Hit: 23 (3d10 + 7) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, they are knocked prone.

Actions.Name: Lay the Foundation
Actions.Content: The builder pours wet concrete on the ground in a 20-foot square starting from a point within 5 feet of the builder. Until the foundation dries in 1 hour, that area is difficult terrain, and when a creature with a Strength score of 20 or less starts their turn in that area, they must succeed on a DC 19 Strength (Athletics) check or be restrained until the start of their next turn.

Creatures.s6jgjeic.Name: Jungle Guide

Traits.Name: Guided Step (3/day)
Traits.Content: As a Bonus Action, the guide calls out quick instructions. One ally within 30 ft. can move up to 10 ft. without provoking opportunity attacks and gains +2 AC against the next attack before the start of the guide’s next turn.

Traits.Name: Mark of the Trail (3/day)
Traits.Content: As an Action, the guide marks or distracts beasts in a 10-ft. radius zone for 1 minute. Enemies must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom save or have disadvantage on their next attack. Allies making Stealth or Survival checks in or near the zone have advantage.

Traits.Name: Spirit Lantern (1/day)
Traits.Content: As an Action, the guide activates a glowing totem for 10 minutes in a 20-ft. radius. Allies in the aura have advantage on saves vs. frightened or poisoned and cannot become lost by nonmagical means.

Actions.Name: Machete
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6+3) slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Axe
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6+3) slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Throwing Knife
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4+3) piercing damage.

Creatures.s6jgjeic.Description: A seasoned pathfinder who reads the jungle like a living map, guiding travelers through its dangers with calm precision.

Creatures.sb6tn6qv.Name: Force of Blood
Creatures.sb6tn6qv.Type: Large Elemental (Earth, Fire, Water), Any Alignment

Traits.Name: Visceral Sight
Traits.Content: The force of blood can see any creature within 120 feet of them who isn’t a Construct or an Undead. This sight ignores effects that obscure sight.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The force of blood makes three Scarlet Longspear attacks. They can replace one attack with a Rend from Within attack.

Actions.Name: Scarlet Longspear
Actions.Content: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +13 to hit, reach 15 ft. or range 30/60 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (2d10 + 7) piercing damage. If the target is a creature, the force of blood regains 5 hit points.

Actions.Name: Rend from Within
Actions.Content: Melee Spell Attack: +12 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 13 (2d6 + 6) slashing damage plus 26 (4d12) necrotic damage, and the target spends 1 Hit Die with no effect and can’t take reactions until the start of their next turn. Miss: 13 (2d12) necrotic damage.

BonusActions.Name: Convocation of Blood
BonusActions.Content:
The force of blood imbues the power of blood in themself or another Elemental they can see within 30 feet of them, granting one of the following effects of that Elemental’s choice:

**Boiling Blood.** The Elemental boils the blood of a creature within 5 feet of them who isn’t a Construct or an Undead. The target must make a DC 19 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target must choose to either take 27 (5d10) fire damage or make an attack with a weapon that has a damage die of 1d4 or greater against a creature of the Elemental’s choice. If the attack hits, it deals an extra 11 (2d10) fire damage.

**Essence Thief.** The Elemental steals the essence of two creatures the Elemental can see within 30 feet of them. Each target must make a DC 19 Constitution saving throw, taking 19 (3d12) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The Elemental can then teleport to an unoccupied space they can see within 5 feet of one of the targets.

Traits.Name: Amphibious
Traits.Content: The seer can breathe air and water.

Traits.Name: Third Eye
Traits.Content: Creatures within 30 feet of the seer can’t hide from the seer. Creatures who can’t be targeted by divination magic are immune to this effect.

Traits.Name: Toxiferous
Traits.Content: When a creature hits the seer with a melee attack while within 5 feet of them or touches the seer, that creature takes 2 (1d4) poison damage.

Actions.Name: Acid Grasp
Actions.Content: Melee Spell Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (4d6) acid damage. If the target is a creature and they attack the seer before the start of the seer’s next turn, the target takes 14 (4d6) acid damage.

Actions.Name: Refulgent Beam
Actions.Content: The seer fires two magical beams of radiant energy, each beam targeting a creature the seer can see within 120 feet of them. Each target must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take 13 (3d8) radiant damage and shed dim light in a 10-foot radius for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn). While shedding light in this way, attack rolls against the target have advantage, the target can’t benefit from being invisible, and they can’t take the Hide action.

Actions.Name: Noxious Bubble (1/Day)
Actions.Content: The seer creates a 5-foot-radius sphere filled with toxic gas in an unoccupied space they can see within 60 feet of them. If undisturbed, this bubble lasts for 1 minute then dissipates harmlessly. If a creature attacks the bubble, touches it, enters its space, or otherwise disturbs it, the bubble bursts, and each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw, taking 27 (6d8) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Actions.Name: Psychedelic Drone (1/Day)
Actions.Content: Each enemy within 60 feet of the seer who can hear them must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take 10 (3d6) psychic damage and be dazed for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn).

BonusActions.Name: Hop
BonusActions.Content: The seer jumps a number of feet up to their walking speed.

Traits.Name: Assassinate
Traits.Content: During his first turn, Scarlet has advantage on attack rolls against any creature who hasn’t taken a turn. The first hit Scarlet scores against a surprised creature is a critical hit.

Traits.Name: Sneak Attack (1/Turn)
Traits.Content: Scarlet deals an extra 14 (4d6) damage when he hits a target with a weapon attack and has advantage on the attack roll, or when the target is within 5 feet of an ally of Scarlet who isn’t incapacitated and Scarlet doesn’t have disadvantage on the attack roll.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: Scarlet makes three Shadow Blade or three Shortbow attacks.

Actions.Name: Shadow Blade
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d6 + 7) necrotic damage. If a target is hit by two or more Shadow Blade attacks on a turn, they must succeed on a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw or be blinded until the end of their next turn.

Actions.Name: Longbow
Actions.Content: Ranged Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (1d8 + 7) piercing damage.

Actions.Name: Razor Garrote
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (1d8 + 7) slashing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, they are grappled (escape DC 17). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, they take 22 (5d8) slashing damage at the start of each of their turns, and Scarlet can’t attack another target.

Actions.Name: Umbral Breath (Recharge 6)
Actions.Content: Scarlet exhales dark flame in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 28 (8d6) necrotic damage and is frightened of Scarlet for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn). On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn’t frightened.

BonusActions.Name: Cunning Action
BonusActions.Content: Scarlet takes the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.

Creatures.sgb25483.Name: Bredbeddle
Senses: blindsight 60 ft. (blind beyond this radius in true form)

Traits.Name: Headless (True Form Only)
Traits.Content: The bredbeddle can’t be blinded or deafened.

Traits.Name: Rejuvenation
Traits.Content: If the bredbeddle dies, they return to life in 1d6 months with all their hit points. If their body was destroyed, it reforms in an unoccupied space nearest to where they died. Only a wish spell or finding and destroying the bredbeddle’s true head can prevent this trait from functioning.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The bredbeddle makes two Executioner’s Axe attacks.

Actions.Name: Executioner’s Axe
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 23 (3d10 + 7) slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Beheading Strike (Recharge 5–6)
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 15 ft., one Large or smaller Giant or Humanoid with a head. Hit: 40 (6d10 + 7) slashing damage, and the target must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the bredbeddle cuts off the target’s head, which lands at the target’s feet. A creature beheaded in this way remains alive unless reduced to 0 hit points, but they are blinded and deafened, and they can’t speak. A creature can use an action to place a beheaded creature’s head back on their neck, reattaching the head and ending the effect. A beheaded creature dies if they are separated from their head for more than 24 hours.

BonusActions.Name: Get a Head
BonusActions.Content:
The bredbeddle picks up a severed Giant or Humanoid head within 15 feet of them and places it on the bredbeddle’s neck. The bredbeddle then polymorphs into that Giant or Humanoid creature. While polymorphed, the bredbeddle’s statistics change as follows:

- They can speak.
- They lose their Headless trait.
- Their size changes to match the creature whose head they bear.
- They can see normally, and they gain any special senses the creature’s head had.
- Any equipment they are wearing or carrying temporarily changes to match their size, but the equipment’s statistics do not change.

The bredbeddle reverts to their true form if they die, if their head is removed, or after 24 hours. When they revert, the head falls off and can’t be reattached to the bredbeddle. A creature who can reach the bredbeddle can use an action to make a DC 18 Strength (Athletics) check, removing the head from the bredbeddle on a success.

BonusActions.Name: Toss Head (Polymorphed Form Only)
BonusActions.Content: The bredbeddle removes their head, reverting to their true form, and throws the head at a creature they can see within 60 feet of them. The head lands in the target’s space, and the target must succeed on a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw or take 18 (2d10 + 7) bludgeoning damage.

Traits.Name: False Appearance
Traits.Content: While the willow remains motionless, they are indistinguishable from a normal willow tree.

Traits.Name: Flammable
Traits.Content: If the willow takes fire damage, they catch fire and take 7 (2d6) fire damage at the start of each of their turns for 1 minute. The effect ends early if the willow takes cold damage or a creature uses an action to extinguish the flames.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The willow makes two Frenzied Branch attacks.

Actions.Name: Frenzied Branch
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 30 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) bludgeoning damage. If the willow is on fire, this attack deals an extra 3 (1d6) fire damage.

Actions.Name: Tugging Roots
Actions.Content: Roots erupt from a point on the ground the willow can see within 60 feet of them. Each creature in a 10-foot sphere centered on that point must succeed on a DC 14 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone and moved up to 30 feet toward the willow, taking 4 (1d8) bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet moved.

Actions.Name: Willow’s Tears
Actions.Content: The willow showers salty droplets on their bower. Each creature within 15 feet of the willow must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by the willow (save ends at end of turn). While charmed in this way, a creature can’t willingly move more than 15 feet away from the willow. If a creature charmed in this way takes damage, the effect ends for them.

Reactions.Name: Tree Transformation
Reactions.Content: When a creature within 5 feet of the willow deals slashing damage to the willow, the willow attempts to turn the creature into a tree. The target must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or their feet begin to transform into tree roots and they are restrained. A creature restrained in this way must repeat the saving throw at the end of their next turn. On a successful save, the effect ends. On a failed save, they are transformed into a wooden tree and are petrified. A creature petrified in this way resembles a normal tree of the same size as that creature, except the creature’s face appears carved into the trunk. If the willow dies, the petrification ends and the creature reverts to their true form. A cure ailment power or lesser restoration spell also ends this effect.

Traits.Name: Charm Resistant
Traits.Content: Treyvan has advantage on saving throws he makes to avoid or end the charmed condition on himself.

Traits.Name: Mind Link
Traits.Content: Treyvan can communicate telepathically with any Amethyst Knife boss regardless of distance, provided they are on the same plane of existence.

Traits.Name: Telekinetic Bastion
Traits.Content: Each ally within 20 feet of Treyvan has a +2 bonus to AC and Dexterity saving throws. Enemies treat the area within 20 feet of Treyvan as difficult terrain.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: Treyvan makes two Maul attacks. He can replace one attack with a use of Telekinetic Throw or Choke, if available.

Actions.Name: Maul
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d6 + 6) bludgeoning damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, Treyvan can choose to either knock them prone or push them up to 10 feet away from him.

Actions.Name: Telekinetic Throw
Actions.Content: Treyvan throws his maul with telekinetic force, making a Maul attack with a range of 30 feet. After making this attack, the maul immediately returns to Treyvan’s hands.

Actions.Name: Choke (2/Day; 5th-Order Power; Concentration)
Actions.Content: Treyvan telekinetically crushes one Large or smaller creature he can see within 30 feet of him. The target must succeed on a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw or be restrained for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn). While restrained in this way, the target takes 14 (4d6) force damage at the start of each of their turns. If the target is ever more than 30 feet away from Treyvan or has total cover from him, the power ends early.

Reactions.Name: Shared Condition
Reactions.Content: When Treyvan is blinded, charmed, dazed, deafened, frightened, paralyzed, or stunned, he chooses a willing Amethyst Knife boss within 60 feet of him who doesn’t already have the condition or is immune to it. The chosen creature then gains the condition for the duration, and Treyvan loses the condition. He can use this reaction even while incapacitated.

Traits.Name: Steady
Traits.Content: The griffon has advantage on ability checks and saving throws against being knocked prone.

Traits.Name: Swoop
Traits.Content: If the griffon flies more than 20 feet in a straight line toward their target and then hits them with a Claw attack on the same turn, the target is grappled (escape DC 14). While the target is grappled in this way, the griffon has advantage on Beak attacks against the target but can’t make Claw attacks. Grappling a Medium or smaller creature imposes no penalty on the griffon’s speed.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The griffon makes one Beak attack, and they can make one Claw attack, use Buffet, or use Piercing Cry.

Actions.Name: Beak
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage.

Actions.Name: Claw
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (2d4 + 4) slashing damage.

Actions.Name: Buffet
Actions.Content: The griffon beats their wings in mighty gusts of wind. Each creature not grappled by the griffon within a 30-foot cone originating from the griffon must make a DC 14 Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 5 (2d4) bludgeoning damage, is pushed 5 feet away from the griffon, and is knocked prone.

Actions.Name: Piercing Cry
Actions.Content: The griffon lets out an earsplitting screech. Each enemy within 60 feet of the griffon who can hear them must succeed on a DC 12 Wisdom saving throw or be frightened of the griffon for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn). If a creature’s saving throw is successful or the effect ends for them, they are immune to the Piercing Cry of all griffons for the next 24 hours.

Creatures.srvffqnv.Name: Sylphise
DamageResistances: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from mundane attacks

Traits.Name: Fey Countenance (1/Turn)
Traits.Content: When a creature who can see Sylphise targets her with an attack, power, or spell, the creature must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or be blinded until the start of their next turn (no action required).

Traits.Name: Speak with Beasts and Plants
Traits.Content: Sylphise can communicate with Beasts and Plants as if they shared a language.

Traits.Name: Supernatural Resistance
Traits.Content: Sylphise has advantage on saving throws against powers, spells, and other supernatural effects.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: Sylphise makes three Claw attacks or uses Sibilant Whispers twice.

Actions.Name: Claw
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d6 + 5) slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) psychic damage.

Actions.Name: Sibilant Whispers
Actions.Content: One creature Sylphise can see within 60 feet of her must succeed on a DC 16 Charisma saving throw or take 10 (3d6) psychic damage.

Actions.Name: Etherealness
Actions.Content: Sylphise magically enters the Ethereal Plane from the Mundane World, or vice versa.

Reactions.Name: Too Slow!
Reactions.Content: If a creature misses Sylphise with an attack, she can move up to her speed without provoking opportunity attacks from that creature.

Traits.Name: Minion
Traits.Content: If the goon takes damage from an attack or as the result of a failed saving throw, their hit points are reduced to 0. If the goon takes damage from another effect, they die if the damage equals or exceeds their hit point maximum; otherwise they take no damage.

Actions.Name: Fist (Group Attack)
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 2 bludgeoning damage, and if this attack was made by more than one goon, the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be knocked prone and moved up to 10 feet horizontally. The DC for this saving throw equals 10 plus the number of goons who joined the attack.

Creatures.sy7nzph0.Name: Servok Miner
DamageResistances: bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from mundane attacks

Traits.Name: Immutable Form
Traits.Content: The miner is immune to any power, spell, or effect that would alter their form.

Traits.Name: Supernatural Resistance
Traits.Content: The miner has advantage on saving throws against powers, spells, and other supernatural effects.

Traits.Name: Tunneler
Traits.Content: The miner can burrow through solid rock at half their burrowing speed and leaves a 5-foot-diameter tunnel in their wake.

Actions.Name: Multiattack
Actions.Content: The miner makes two attacks using Drill Press, Pneumatic Pick, or both.

Actions.Name: Drill Press
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) piercing damage. If the target is touching the ground, they are knocked prone and restrained by the miner’s drill. While restrained in this way, the target takes 15 (2d8 + 6) piercing damage at the start of each of their turns, and the miner can’t make Drill Press attacks. The restrained condition ends if the miner moves, makes a Drill Press attack, or is destroyed. The target or a creature who can reach them can use an action to pull the target away from the drill, ending the restrained condition and dealing 15 (2d8 + 6) piercing damage to the target in the process.

Actions.Name: Pneumatic Pick
Actions.Content: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (2d10 + 6) piercing damage. If the target is wearing mundane armor, their armor takes a permanent and cumulative −1 penalty to the AC it offers. Armor reduced to an AC of 10 is destroyed.

Actions.Name: Unload Rocks
Actions.Content: The miner hurls rocks and dirt in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 44 (8d10) bludgeoning damage and is blinded until the end of their next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and is not blinded.

BonusActions.Name: Break Ground
BonusActions.Content: The miner strikes the ground and creates a fissure extending from them in a 30-foot-long, 5-foot-wide, 30-foot-deep line. Each Medium or smaller creature in that area must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature falls into the fissure, taking 10 (3d6) bludgeoning damage from the fall and landing prone. On a successful save, a creature is shunted 5 feet into an unoccupied space and does not fall into the fissure. If a creature doesn’t have an unoccupied space within 5 feet of them, they fall into the fissure.

Traits.Name: Empower Patron
Traits.Content: A non-minion creature serves as the mage’s patron in exchange for the mage’s protective magic. At the start of the patron’s turn, the patron gains temporary hit points equal to twice the number of apprentice mages within 60 feet of them who chose them as a patron and can see them.

Traits.Name: Exploit Weakness
Traits.Content: When the mage makes or joins an attack that’s made with advantage, the attack deals an extra 1 damage per mage who made or joined the attack.

Traits.Name: Minion
Traits.Content: If the mage takes damage from an attack or as the result of a failed saving throw, their hit points are reduced to 0. If the mage takes damage from another effect, they die if the damage equals or exceeds their hit point maximum; otherwise they take no damage.

Actions.Name: Lightning Strike (Group Attack)
